`source/apps/CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole/README.md`:

```md
# CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole

**[EN](#english) · [RU / Русский](#русский)**

---

## English

User-mode console to manage `CS2IsValveDSSpooferDriver.sys`. Interactively reads/writes values to SHM `\BaseNamedObjects\IsValveDSState`, shows live status of the kernel worker.

### Version-independence

The console contains no client.dll offsets — everything works through the driver's SHM API. Fully compatible with any CS2 version supported by the driver.

### Build

```powershell
& "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community\MSBuild\Current\Bin\MSBuild.exe" `
  source\apps\CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole\CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole.vcxproj `
  /p:Configuration=Release /p:Platform=x64
```

Output: `x64\Release\CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole.exe` (~195 KB).

### Usage

```
CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole.exe
> h        # help
> 0        # set m_bIsValveDS = 0 (community server)
> 1        # set m_bIsValveDS = 1 (Valve DS)
> s        # status
> q        # quit (signal worker stop)
```

### Named objects (shared with driver)

| Type | Name (Win32) | Kernel path |
|---|---|---|
| SECTION | `Global\IsValveDSState` | `\BaseNamedObjects\IsValveDSState` |
| EVENT | `Global\IsValveDSStop` | `\BaseNamedObjects\IsValveDSStop` |
| EVENT | `Global\IsValveDSStopped` | `\BaseNamedObjects\IsValveDSStopped` |

All definitions in `shared.h` (identical copy in `drivers/CS2IsValveDSSpooferDriver/shared.h`).

---

## Русский

User-mode консоль для управления `CS2IsValveDSSpooferDriver.sys`. Интерактивно читает/пишет значения в SHM `\BaseNamedObjects\IsValveDSState`, показывает live-статус kernel worker'а.

### Version-независимость

Консоль не содержит client.dll оффсетов — всё работает через SHM API драйвера. Полностью совместима с любой версией CS2, которую поддерживает драйвер.

### Сборка

```powershell
& "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community\MSBuild\Current\Bin\MSBuild.exe" `
  source\apps\CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole\CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole.vcxproj `
  /p:Configuration=Release /p:Platform=x64
```

Выход: `x64\Release\CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole.exe` (~195 KB).

### Использование

```
CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole.exe
> h        # help
> 0        # set m_bIsValveDS = 0 (community server)
> 1        # set m_bIsValveDS = 1 (Valve DS)
> s        # status
> q        # quit (signal worker stop)
```

### Named objects (общие с драйвером)

| Тип | Имя (Win32) | Kernel-путь |
|---|---|---|
| SECTION | `Global\IsValveDSState` | `\BaseNamedObjects\IsValveDSState` |
| EVENT | `Global\IsValveDSStop` | `\BaseNamedObjects\IsValveDSStop` |
| EVENT | `Global\IsValveDSStopped` | `\BaseNamedObjects\IsValveDSStopped` |

Все definitions в `shared.h` (идентичная копия в `drivers/CS2IsValveDSSpooferDriver/shared.h`).

```

`source/apps/CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole/main.cpp`:

```cpp
// CS2 IsValveDS Spoofer console v1.14 — interactive TUI (v2 protocol). Freeze mode + presets + auto-toggle.
//
// Full-screen VT100 TUI that talks to IsValveDS_Driver.sys via SHM
// (\BaseNamedObjects\IsValveDSState). Replaces the previous line-oriented
// REPL. Adds freeze mode (v2 protocol), preset picker, and an auto-toggle
// worker thread. Config JSON lives in
// %LOCALAPPDATA%\CS2IsValveDSSpoofer\config.json.
//
// Only depends on kernel32 + CRT (/MT). No IRPs, no dbghelp, no logging file.

#define WIN32_LEAN_AND_MEAN
#include <windows.h>

#include <cstdio>
#include <cstring>
#include <cstdarg>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <conio.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>

#include "shared.h"

// --- VT100 / SGR / box drawing ----------------------------------------------
#define CSI            "\x1b["
#define VT_CLEAR_ALL   CSI "2J" CSI "H"
#define VT_HOME_ERASE  CSI "H"  CSI "J"     // home + erase-below (no scroll)
#define VT_HIDE        CSI "?25l"
#define VT_SHOW        CSI "?25h"
#define R_RESET        CSI "0m"
#define R_DIM          CSI "2m"
#define R_YELLOW       CSI "33m"
#define R_CYAN         CSI "36m"
#define R_GRAY         CSI "90m"
#define R_BR_RED       CSI "91m"
#define R_BR_GREEN     CSI "92m"
#define R_BR_YELLOW    CSI "93m"
#define R_BR_CYAN      CSI "96m"

// Box-drawing (UTF-8; each 1 visual column when console CP=UTF-8).
static const char* BOX_H  = "\xE2\x94\x80"; // ─
static const char* BOX_V  = "\xE2\x94\x82"; // │
static const char* BOX_TL = "\xE2\x94\x8C"; // ┌
static const char* BOX_TR = "\xE2\x94\x90"; // ┐
static const char* BOX_BL = "\xE2\x94\x94"; // └
static const char* BOX_BR = "\xE2\x94\x98"; // ┘
static const char* BOX_LM = "\xE2\x94\x9C"; // ├
static const char* BOX_RM = "\xE2\x94\xA4"; // ┤

static constexpr int TOTAL_WIDTH = 80;
static constexpr int INNER_WIDTH = TOTAL_WIDTH - 2;

// --- Globals ----------------------------------------------------------------
static HANDLE                    g_shmHandle  = nullptr;
static ISVALVEDS_STATE*          g_state      = nullptr;
static HANDLE                    g_stopEvent  = nullptr;
static HANDLE                    g_doneEvent  = nullptr;
static std::atomic<bool>         g_quit       { false };
static std::atomic<unsigned int> g_nextReqId  { 1 };
static std::atomic<int>          g_refreshHz  { 4 };
static std::atomic<bool>         g_autoRun    { false };
static std::atomic<int>          g_autoNext   { 1 };
static std::thread               g_autoThread;

struct LastWrite {
    bool         valid     = false;
    unsigned int reqId     = 0;
    unsigned int handledId = 0;
    unsigned int err       = 0;
    int          readback  = -1;
};
static LastWrite g_lastWrite;

// Transient status line (fades after ~5s).
static char        g_statusBuf[192] = {};
static const char* g_statusColor    = R_GRAY;
static DWORD       g_statusTick     = 0;

// Snapshot of SHM used by the renderer.
struct StateSnap {
    bool               valid;
    unsigned int       magic;
    unsigned int       protocol;
    int                current_value;
    unsigned int       current_error;
    unsigned long long current_address;
    unsigned long long client_base;
    unsigned long long last_poll_systime;
    unsigned int       driver_tick;
    unsigned int       cs2_pid;
    unsigned int       desired_value;
    unsigned int       write_request_id;
    unsigned int       write_handled_id;
    unsigned int       write_error;
    int                write_result_value;
    unsigned long long write_handled_systime;
    unsigned int       freeze_enabled;
    unsigned int       freeze_value;
    unsigned long long freeze_writes;
    unsigned int       freeze_errors;
};

// --- Utilities --------------------------------------------------------------
static const char* ErrToString(unsigned int e)
{
    switch (e) {
    case VDS_ERR_OK:             return "OK";
    case VDS_ERR_NO_CS2:         return "no cs2";
    case VDS_ERR_NO_CLIENT_DLL:  return "no client.dll";
    case VDS_ERR_GAMERULES_NULL: return "GameRules NULL";
    case VDS_ERR_READ_FAILED:    return "read failed";
    case VDS_ERR_WRITE_FAILED:   return "write failed";
    case VDS_ERR_BAD_VALUE:      return "bad value";
    case VDS_ERR_TIMEOUT:        return "timeout";
    default:                     return "unknown";
    }
}

static void SetStatus(const char* color, const char* fmt, ...)
{
    va_list ap; va_start(ap, fmt);
    _vsnprintf_s(g_statusBuf, sizeof(g_statusBuf), _TRUNCATE, fmt, ap);
    va_end(ap);
    g_statusColor = color ? color : R_GRAY;
    g_statusTick  = GetTickCount();
}

// Read whole SHM with torn-read protection (magic before + after, retry).
static bool ReadSnap(StateSnap& s)
{
    s = {};
    if (!g_state) return false;
    for (int attempt = 0; attempt < 8; attempt++) {
        unsigned int m1 = g_state->magic;
        if (m1 != ISVALVEDS_MAGIC) { Sleep(1); continue; }
        _ReadWriteBarrier();
        s.protocol              = g_state->protocol_version;
        s.current_value         = g_state->current_value;
        s.current_error         = g_state->current_error;
        s.current_address       = g_state->current_address;
        s.client_base           = g_state->client_base;
        s.last_poll_systime     = g_state->last_poll_systime;
        s.driver_tick           = g_state->driver_tick;
        s.cs2_pid               = g_state->cs2_pid;
        s.desired_value         = g_state->desired_value;
        s.write_request_id      = g_state->write_request_id;
        s.write_handled_id      = g_state->write_handled_id;
        s.write_error           = g_state->write_error;
        s.write_result_value    = g_state->write_result_value;
        s.write_handled_systime = g_state->write_handled_systime;
        s.freeze_enabled        = g_state->freeze_enabled;
        s.freeze_value          = g_state->freeze_value;
        s.freeze_writes         = g_state->freeze_writes;
        s.freeze_errors         = g_state->freeze_errors;
        _ReadWriteBarrier();
        unsigned int m2 = g_state->magic;
        if (m1 == m2 && m2 == ISVALVEDS_MAGIC) {
            s.magic = m1;
            s.valid = true;
            return true;
        }
        Sleep(1);
    }
    return false;
}

// --- SHM writes -------------------------------------------------------------
static bool RequestOneShotWrite(unsigned int desired, unsigned int timeoutMs)
{
    if (!g_state) return false;
    unsigned int reqId = g_nextReqId.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_relaxed);
    if (reqId == 0) reqId = g_nextReqId.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_relaxed);

    InterlockedExchange(reinterpret_cast<volatile LONG*>(&g_state->desired_value),
                        desired ? 1L : 0L);
    _ReadWriteBarrier();
    InterlockedExchange(reinterpret_cast<volatile LONG*>(&g_state->write_request_id),
                        static_cast<LONG>(reqId));

    g_lastWrite.valid     = true;
    g_lastWrite.reqId     = reqId;
    g_lastWrite.handledId = 0;
    g_lastWrite.err       = 0;
    g_lastWrite.readback  = -1;

    DWORD start = GetTickCount();
    while (GetTickCount() - start < timeoutMs) {
        unsigned int handled = g_state->write_handled_id;
        if (handled == reqId) {
            g_lastWrite.handledId = handled;
            g_lastWrite.err       = g_state->write_error;
            g_lastWrite.readback  = g_state->write_result_value;
            return true;
        }
        Sleep(15);
    }
    g_lastWrite.handledId = g_state->write_handled_id;
    g_lastWrite.err       = VDS_ERR_TIMEOUT;
    g_lastWrite.readback  = -1;
    return false;
}

static void SetFreezeEnabled(unsigned int on)
{
    if (!g_state) return;
    _ReadWriteBarrier();
    InterlockedExchange(reinterpret_cast<volatile LONG*>(&g_state->freeze_enabled),
                        on ? 1L : 0L);
}

static void SetFreezeValue(unsigned int v)
{
    if (!g_state) return;
    _ReadWriteBarrier();
    InterlockedExchange(reinterpret_cast<volatile LONG*>(&g_state->freeze_value),
                        v ? 1L : 0L);
}

static void PublishDesiredNoBump(unsigned int v)
{
    if (!g_state) return;
    _ReadWriteBarrier();
    InterlockedExchange(reinterpret_cast<volatile LONG*>(&g_state->desired_value),
                        v ? 1L : 0L);
}

// --- Auto-toggle worker -----------------------------------------------------
static void AutoToggleThread()
{
    bool  wasRunning = false;
    DWORD lastFire   = 0;
    while (!g_quit.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)) {
        bool run = g_autoRun.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
        if (run && !wasRunning) {
            lastFire = GetTickCount() - 5000; // fire immediately on start
        }
        wasRunning = run;
        if (run && (GetTickCount() - lastFire) >= 5000) {
            int cur  = g_autoNext.load();
            int next = cur ? 0 : 1;
            g_autoNext.store(next);
            lastFire = GetTickCount();
            RequestOneShotWrite(static_cast<unsigned>(cur), 2000);
        }
        Sleep(100);
    }
}

static void AutoToggleStart()
{
    g_autoNext.store(1);
    g_autoRun.store(true);
}

static void AutoToggleStop()
{
    g_autoRun.store(false);
}

// --- Config JSON ------------------------------------------------------------
static bool ConfigDir(char* out, size_t cap)
{
    char base[MAX_PATH] = {};
    if (!GetEnvironmentVariableA("LOCALAPPDATA", base, MAX_PATH)) return false;
    _snprintf_s(out, cap, _TRUNCATE, "%s\\CS2IsValveDSSpoofer", base);
    return true;
}

static bool ConfigPath(char* out, size_t cap)
{
    char dir[MAX_PATH] = {};
    if (!ConfigDir(dir, sizeof(dir))) return false;
    _snprintf_s(out, cap, _TRUNCATE, "%s\\config.json", dir);
    return true;
}

static bool SaveConfig(const StateSnap& s, char* pathOut, size_t pathCap)
{
    char dir[MAX_PATH] = {};
    if (!ConfigDir(dir, sizeof(dir))) return false;
    CreateDirectoryA(dir, nullptr); // OK if already exists
    char path[MAX_PATH] = {};
    if (!ConfigPath(path, sizeof(path))) return false;
    FILE* fp = nullptr;
    if (fopen_s(&fp, path, "wb") != 0 || !fp) return false;
    fprintf(fp,
        "{\r\n"
        "  \"freeze_enabled\": %u,\r\n"
        "  \"freeze_value\":   %u,\r\n"
        "  \"desired_value\":  %u\r\n"
        "}\r\n",
        s.freeze_enabled ? 1u : 0u,
        s.freeze_value   ? 1u : 0u,
        s.desired_value  ? 1u : 0u);
    fclose(fp);
    if (pathOut) strcpy_s(pathOut, pathCap, path);
    return true;
}

// Tiny JSON pull: find "key" then next non-negative integer literal.
static int JsonInt(const char* text, const char* key, int fallback)
{
    const char* p = strstr(text, key);
    if (!p) return fallback;
    p += strlen(key);
    while (*p && *p != ':') p++;
    if (!*p) return fallback;
    p++;
    while (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t') p++;
    if (*p < '0' || *p > '9') return fallback;
    return atoi(p);
}

static bool LoadConfig(unsigned int& fe, unsigned int& fv, unsigned int& dv,
                       char* pathOut, size_t pathCap)
{
    char path[MAX_PATH] = {};
    if (!ConfigPath(path, sizeof(path))) return false;
    FILE* fp = nullptr;
    if (fopen_s(&fp, path, "rb") != 0 || !fp) return false;
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_END);
    long sz = ftell(fp);
    fseek(fp, 0, SEEK_SET);
    if (sz <= 0 || sz > 4096) { fclose(fp); return false; }
    char buf[4096] = {};
    fread(buf, 1, (size_t)sz, fp);
    fclose(fp);
    fe = JsonInt(buf, "\"freeze_enabled\"", 0) ? 1u : 0u;
    fv = JsonInt(buf, "\"freeze_value\"",   0) ? 1u : 0u;
    dv = JsonInt(buf, "\"desired_value\"",  0) ? 1u : 0u;
    if (pathOut) strcpy_s(pathOut, pathCap, path);
    return true;
}

// --- Console setup / rendering primitives -----------------------------------
static void EnableVT()
{
    SetConsoleOutputCP(CP_UTF8);
    HANDLE h = GetStdHandle(STD_OUTPUT_HANDLE);
    if (!h || h == INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) return;
    DWORD mode = 0;
    if (!GetConsoleMode(h, &mode)) return;
    mode |= ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_PROCESSING | ENABLE_PROCESSED_OUTPUT;
    SetConsoleMode(h, mode);

    // Try to widen the buffer so the 80-column frame doesn't wrap on narrow
    // consoles. Best-effort; SetConsoleScreenBufferSize can fail if a window
    // dimension exceeds requested buffer.
    CONSOLE_SCREEN_BUFFER_INFO csbi{};
    if (GetConsoleScreenBufferInfo(h, &csbi) && csbi.dwSize.X < TOTAL_WIDTH) {
        COORD sz{ TOTAL_WIDTH, csbi.dwSize.Y };
        SetConsoleScreenBufferSize(h, sz);
    }
}

// Copy `piece` `count` times into `out` (no trailing anything).
static void RepeatChars(char* out, size_t outCap, const char* piece, int count)
{
    size_t plen = strlen(piece);
    size_t pos = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
        if (pos + plen + 1 >= outCap) break;
        memcpy(out + pos, piece, plen);
        pos += plen;
    }
    out[pos] = 0;
}

// left + " title " padded with box-H dashes + right, total = TOTAL_WIDTH.
static void PrintTitleBorder(const char* left, const char* right, const char* title)
{
    int titleLen  = title ? (int)strlen(title) : 0;
    int textCells = titleLen ? (titleLen + 2) : 0; // spaces around title
    int leadDash  = titleLen ? 4 : INNER_WIDTH;
    int trailDash = INNER_WIDTH - leadDash - textCells;
    if (trailDash < 0) { leadDash = 0; trailDash = INNER_WIDTH - textCells; }
    if (trailDash < 0) { trailDash = 0; }
    char lead[512], trail[512];
    RepeatChars(lead,  sizeof(lead),  BOX_H, leadDash);
    RepeatChars(trail, sizeof(trail), BOX_H, trailDash);
    if (titleLen) {
        printf("%s%s %s %s%s\n", left, lead, title, trail, right);
    } else {
        printf("%s%s%s\n", left, lead, right);
    }
}

static void PrintPlainBorder(const char* left, const char* right)
{
    char line[512];
    RepeatChars(line, sizeof(line), BOX_H, INNER_WIDTH);
    printf("%s%s%s\n", left, line, right);
}

// Row of pure-ASCII content, padded with spaces to inner width.
static void RowAscii(const char* fmt, ...)
{
    char buf[512];
    va_list ap; va_start(ap, fmt);
    _vsnprintf_s(buf, sizeof(buf), _TRUNCATE, fmt, ap);
    va_end(ap);
    int len = (int)strlen(buf);
    if (len > INNER_WIDTH) len = INNER_WIDTH;
    printf("%s%.*s%*s%s\n", BOX_V, len, buf, INNER_WIDTH - len, "", BOX_V);
}

// Row with an SGR-colored segment sandwiched between plain prefix and suffix.
// All three inputs must be pure ASCII (no escape codes) so visible width is
// exactly strlen; the SGR sequences we insert take zero visual columns.
static void RowColored(const char* prefix, const char* color,
                       const char* colored, const char* suffix)
{
    int prefLen = prefix  ? (int)strlen(prefix)  : 0;
    int colLen  = colored ? (int)strlen(colored) : 0;
    int sufLen  = suffix  ? (int)strlen(suffix)  : 0;
    int visible = prefLen + colLen + sufLen;
    if (visible > INNER_WIDTH) visible = INNER_WIDTH;
    int pad = INNER_WIDTH - visible;
    if (pad < 0) pad = 0;
    printf("%s%s%s%s%s%s%*s%s\n",
           BOX_V,
           prefix  ? prefix  : "",
           color   ? color   : "",
           colored ? colored : "",
           R_RESET,
           suffix  ? suffix  : "",
           pad, "",
           BOX_V);
}

// --- Main TUI render --------------------------------------------------------
static void Render(const StateSnap& s)
{
    // Row 1: driver status
    char drvLine[192];
    if (s.valid) {
        _snprintf_s(drvLine, sizeof(drvLine), _TRUNCATE,
            " Driver: RUNNING    tick=%-8u  cs2.pid=%-6u  protocol=v%u",
            s.driver_tick, s.cs2_pid, s.protocol);
    } else {
        _snprintf_s(drvLine, sizeof(drvLine), _TRUNCATE,
            " Driver: NOT READY  (SHM magic invalid; is the driver loaded?)");
    }
    const char* drvColor = s.valid ? R_GRAY : R_BR_RED;

    // Row 2: base pointers
    char basesLine[192];
    _snprintf_s(basesLine, sizeof(basesLine), _TRUNCATE,
        " client.dll: 0x%016llX     GameRules addr: 0x%016llX",
        (unsigned long long)s.client_base, (unsigned long long)s.current_address);

    // Row 3: current value (colored)
    const char* valColor = R_GRAY;
    char valLabel[96];
    if (!s.valid || s.current_error != VDS_ERR_OK || s.current_value < 0) {
        _snprintf_s(valLabel, sizeof(valLabel), _TRUNCATE,
                    "[?] %s", s.valid ? ErrToString(s.current_error) : "no data");
        valColor = R_GRAY;
    } else if (s.current_value == 1) {
        _snprintf_s(valLabel, sizeof(valLabel), _TRUNCATE,
                    "[1] VALVE DS  (server thinks: OFFICIAL)");
        valColor = R_BR_GREEN;
    } else if (s.current_value == 0) {
        _snprintf_s(valLabel, sizeof(valLabel), _TRUNCATE,
                    "[0] COMMUNITY (server thinks: 3rd-party)");
        valColor = R_BR_RED;
    } else {
        _snprintf_s(valLabel, sizeof(valLabel), _TRUNCATE,
                    "[%d] out-of-range", s.current_value);
        valColor = R_BR_YELLOW;
    }

    // Row 4: address (bare hex)
    char addrLine[128];
    _snprintf_s(addrLine, sizeof(addrLine), _TRUNCATE,
        " Address:  0x%016llX",
        (unsigned long long)s.current_address);

    // Row 5: last poll age
    char pollLine[128];
    if (s.valid && s.last_poll_systime) {
        FILETIME now; GetSystemTimeAsFileTime(&now);
        ULONGLONG nowT = ((ULONGLONG)now.dwHighDateTime << 32) | now.dwLowDateTime;
        long long deltaMs = ((long long)nowT - (long long)s.last_poll_systime) / 10000;
        if (deltaMs < 0) deltaMs = 0;
        _snprintf_s(pollLine, sizeof(pollLine), _TRUNCATE,
            " Last driver poll: %lld ms ago", deltaMs);
    } else {
        _snprintf_s(pollLine, sizeof(pollLine), _TRUNCATE,
            " Last driver poll: -");
    }

    // Freeze summary
    char freezeLine[192];
    _snprintf_s(freezeLine, sizeof(freezeLine), _TRUNCATE,
        " FREEZE: [%s] %s  ->  target=%u (%s)",
        s.freeze_enabled ? "X" : ".",
        s.freeze_enabled ? "ENABLED " : "disabled",
        s.freeze_value,
        s.freeze_value ? "Valve DS" : "Community");
    const char* freezeColor = s.freeze_enabled ? R_BR_CYAN : R_GRAY;

    char freezeLine2[192];
    _snprintf_s(freezeLine2, sizeof(freezeLine2), _TRUNCATE,
        "         re-applies: %-8llu  errors: %u",
        (unsigned long long)s.freeze_writes, s.freeze_errors);
    const char* freezeErrColor = s.freeze_errors ? R_BR_RED : R_GRAY;

    // Last one-shot write
    char wrLine[192];
    if (g_lastWrite.valid) {
        _snprintf_s(wrLine, sizeof(wrLine), _TRUNCATE,
            "  Request id: %-6u  Handled id: %-6u  Result: %-14.14s  Readback: %d",
            g_lastWrite.reqId, g_lastWrite.handledId,
            ErrToString(g_lastWrite.err), g_lastWrite.readback);
    } else {
        _snprintf_s(wrLine, sizeof(wrLine), _TRUNCATE,
            "  (no one-shot write issued this session)");
    }
    const char* wrColor = R_GRAY;
    if (g_lastWrite.valid)
        wrColor = (g_lastWrite.err == VDS_ERR_OK) ? R_BR_GREEN : R_BR_RED;

    // Menu
    char menu1[128], menu2[128], menu3[128], menu4[128];
    _snprintf_s(menu1, sizeof(menu1), _TRUNCATE,
        "  [0] set Community (0)    [1] set Valve DS (1)    [T] toggle current");
    _snprintf_s(menu2, sizeof(menu2), _TRUNCATE,
        "  [F] freeze on/off        [V] flip freeze value   [P] presets");
    _snprintf_s(menu3, sizeof(menu3), _TRUNCATE,
        "  [R] refresh Hz (%2dHz)    [J] save cfg            [L] load cfg",
        g_refreshHz.load());
    _snprintf_s(menu4, sizeof(menu4), _TRUNCATE,
        "  [U] unload driver        [Q] quit                Auto-toggle: %s",
        g_autoRun.load() ? "ON " : "OFF");

    // Status line (transient)
    char statusLine[224];
    const char* statusColor = R_GRAY;
    if (g_statusBuf[0] && (GetTickCount() - g_statusTick) < 5000) {
        _snprintf_s(statusLine, sizeof(statusLine), _TRUNCATE,
                    "  %s", g_statusBuf);
        statusColor = g_statusColor;
    } else {
        _snprintf_s(statusLine, sizeof(statusLine), _TRUNCATE,
                    "  Press a menu key. Q/Esc = quit; driver keeps running.");
    }

    // Emit whole frame
    printf(VT_HIDE VT_HOME_ERASE);
    PrintTitleBorder(BOX_TL, BOX_TR, "CS2 IsValveDS Spoofer v1.14");
    RowColored("", drvColor, drvLine, "");
    RowAscii("%s", basesLine);
    RowColored(" Current value: ", valColor, valLabel, "");
    RowAscii("%s", addrLine);
    RowAscii("%s", pollLine);
    PrintPlainBorder(BOX_LM, BOX_RM);
    RowColored("", freezeColor, freezeLine, "");
    RowColored("", freezeErrColor, freezeLine2, "");
    PrintTitleBorder(BOX_LM, BOX_RM, "Last one-shot write");
    RowColored("", wrColor, wrLine, "");
    PrintPlainBorder(BOX_LM, BOX_RM);
    RowAscii("%s", menu1);
    RowAscii("%s", menu2);
    RowAscii("%s", menu3);
    RowAscii("%s", menu4);
    PrintPlainBorder(BOX_LM, BOX_RM);
    RowColored("", statusColor, statusLine, "");
    PrintPlainBorder(BOX_BL, BOX_BR);

    printf(R_RESET);
    fflush(stdout);
}

// --- Preset submenu (blocking read of one key) ------------------------------
static void ShowPresetsAndApply()
{
    printf(VT_CLEAR_ALL VT_SHOW);
    printf("\n  " R_BR_YELLOW "Select preset:" R_RESET "\n\n");
    printf("    " R_CYAN "1" R_RESET ") Valve DS locked   (freeze=1, value=1)\n");
    printf("    " R_CYAN "2" R_RESET ") Community locked  (freeze=1, value=0)\n");
    printf("    " R_CYAN "3" R_RESET ") Free              (freeze off)\n");
    printf("    " R_CYAN "4" R_RESET ") Auto-toggle every 5s  [%s]\n",
           g_autoRun.load() ? "currently RUNNING; picking again stops it"
                            : "currently stopped");
    printf("\n  " R_DIM "Press 1-4 to pick, any other key to cancel." R_RESET "\n");
    fflush(stdout);

    int c = _getch();
    if (c == 0 || c == 0xE0) (void)_getch();

    switch (c) {
    case '1':
        AutoToggleStop();
        SetFreezeValue(1);
        SetFreezeEnabled(1);
        SetStatus(R_BR_CYAN, "Preset 1: Valve DS locked (freeze=1, val=1)");
        break;
    case '2':
        AutoToggleStop();
        SetFreezeValue(0);
        SetFreezeEnabled(1);
        SetStatus(R_BR_CYAN, "Preset 2: Community locked (freeze=1, val=0)");
        break;
    case '3':
        AutoToggleStop();
        SetFreezeEnabled(0);
        SetStatus(R_BR_GREEN, "Preset 3: Free (freeze off)");
        break;
    case '4':
        SetFreezeEnabled(0);
        if (g_autoRun.load()) {
            AutoToggleStop();
            SetStatus(R_YELLOW, "Auto-toggle stopped.");
        } else {
            AutoToggleStart();
            SetStatus(R_BR_CYAN, "Auto-toggle armed: alternates 0<->1 every 5s.");
        }
        break;
    default:
        SetStatus(R_GRAY, "Preset cancelled.");
        break;
    }
    printf(VT_CLEAR_ALL);
}

// --- Key dispatch -----------------------------------------------------------
static void HandleKey(int c, const StateSnap& lastSnap)
{
    if (c == 0 || c == 0xE0) { (void)_getch(); return; }
    if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z') c = c - 'a' + 'A';

    switch (c) {
    case 'Q':
    case 27: // Esc
        g_quit.store(true);
        return;

    case '0':
        if (RequestOneShotWrite(0, 2000) && g_lastWrite.err == VDS_ERR_OK)
            SetStatus(R_BR_GREEN, "Wrote 0 (Community). Readback=%d",
                      g_lastWrite.readback);
        else
            SetStatus(R_BR_RED, "Write 0 failed: %s (readback=%d)",
                      ErrToString(g_lastWrite.err), g_lastWrite.readback);
        return;

    case '1':
        if (RequestOneShotWrite(1, 2000) && g_lastWrite.err == VDS_ERR_OK)
            SetStatus(R_BR_GREEN, "Wrote 1 (Valve DS). Readback=%d",
                      g_lastWrite.readback);
        else
            SetStatus(R_BR_RED, "Write 1 failed: %s (readback=%d)",
                      ErrToString(g_lastWrite.err), g_lastWrite.readback);
        return;

    case 'T': {
        unsigned int target = 1;
        if (lastSnap.valid && lastSnap.current_error == VDS_ERR_OK &&
            lastSnap.current_value >= 0)
        {
            target = (lastSnap.current_value == 1) ? 0u : 1u;
        }
        if (RequestOneShotWrite(target, 2000) && g_lastWrite.err == VDS_ERR_OK)
            SetStatus(R_BR_GREEN, "Toggled -> %u", target);
        else
            SetStatus(R_BR_RED, "Toggle failed: %s",
                      ErrToString(g_lastWrite.err));
        return;
    }

    case 'F': {
        unsigned int now = lastSnap.freeze_enabled ? 0u : 1u;
        SetFreezeEnabled(now);
        SetStatus(now ? R_BR_CYAN : R_GRAY,
                  "Freeze %s", now ? "ENABLED" : "disabled");
        return;
    }

    case 'V': {
        unsigned int now = lastSnap.freeze_value ? 0u : 1u;
        SetFreezeValue(now);
        SetStatus(R_BR_CYAN, "Freeze target flipped -> %u (%s)",
                  now, now ? "Valve DS" : "Community");
        return;
    }

    case 'P':
        ShowPresetsAndApply();
        return;

    case 'R': {
        int hz = g_refreshHz.load();
        int nx = (hz == 2) ? 4 : (hz == 4) ? 10 : (hz == 10) ? 30 : 2;
        g_refreshHz.store(nx);
        SetStatus(R_YELLOW, "Refresh rate -> %d Hz", nx);
        return;
    }

    case 'J': {
        char path[MAX_PATH] = {};
        if (SaveConfig(lastSnap, path, sizeof(path)))
            SetStatus(R_BR_GREEN, "Saved: %s", path);
        else
            SetStatus(R_BR_RED, "Save failed (check %%LOCALAPPDATA%%)");
        return;
    }

    case 'L': {
        unsigned int fe = 0, fv = 0, dv = 0;
        char path[MAX_PATH] = {};
        if (LoadConfig(fe, fv, dv, path, sizeof(path))) {
            SetFreezeValue(fv);
            SetFreezeEnabled(fe);
            PublishDesiredNoBump(dv); // does not trigger a write on its own
            SetStatus(R_BR_GREEN,
                      "Loaded: freeze_enabled=%u freeze_value=%u desired_value=%u",
                      fe, fv, dv);
        } else {
            SetStatus(R_BR_RED, "Load failed (no config yet or unreadable?)");
        }
        return;
    }

    case 'U':
        if (!g_stopEvent) {
            SetStatus(R_BR_RED, "Stop event unavailable — cannot unload");
            return;
        }
        SetEvent(g_stopEvent);
        SetStatus(R_YELLOW, "Sent stop to driver, waiting up to 2s...");
        if (g_doneEvent) WaitForSingleObject(g_doneEvent, 2000);
        else             Sleep(500);
        g_quit.store(true);
        return;

    default:
        return;
    }
}

// --- CLI backward-compat ----------------------------------------------------
static void PrintStatePlain(const StateSnap& s)
{
    printf("magic=0x%08X protocol=%u\n", s.magic, s.protocol);
    printf("driver_tick=%u cs2_pid=%u client_base=0x%016llX\n",
           s.driver_tick, s.cs2_pid, (unsigned long long)s.client_base);
    printf("current_value=%d current_error=%u (%s)\n",
           s.current_value, s.current_error, ErrToString(s.current_error));
    printf("current_address=0x%016llX\n",
           (unsigned long long)s.current_address);
    printf("desired_value=%u write_request_id=%u\n",
           s.desired_value, s.write_request_id);
    printf("write_handled_id=%u write_error=%u (%s) write_result_value=%d\n",
           s.write_handled_id, s.write_error,
           ErrToString(s.write_error), s.write_result_value);
    printf("freeze_enabled=%u freeze_value=%u\n",
           s.freeze_enabled, s.freeze_value);
    printf("freeze_writes=%llu freeze_errors=%u\n",
           (unsigned long long)s.freeze_writes, s.freeze_errors);
}

// Returns exit code on match, -1 if no CLI flag recognized.
static int CliMode(int argc, char** argv)
{
    for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
        if (!_stricmp(argv[i], "--freeze-on")) {
            if (i + 1 >= argc) { fprintf(stderr, "--freeze-on requires VAL\n"); return 2; }
            unsigned v = (unsigned)atoi(argv[++i]) ? 1u : 0u;
            SetFreezeValue(v);
            SetFreezeEnabled(1);
            printf("Freeze ON, value=%u\n", v);
            return 0;
        }
        if (!_stricmp(argv[i], "--freeze-off")) {
            SetFreezeEnabled(0);
            printf("Freeze OFF\n");
            return 0;
        }
        if (!_stricmp(argv[i], "--set-value")) {
            if (i + 1 >= argc) { fprintf(stderr, "--set-value requires VAL\n"); return 2; }
            unsigned v = (unsigned)atoi(argv[++i]) ? 1u : 0u;
            bool ok = RequestOneShotWrite(v, 2000);
            printf("Set-value %u: %s (err=%s readback=%d)\n",
                   v, ok ? "ACK" : "TIMEOUT",
                   ErrToString(g_lastWrite.err), g_lastWrite.readback);
            return (ok && g_lastWrite.err == VDS_ERR_OK) ? 0 : 1;
        }
        if (!_stricmp(argv[i], "--print-state")) {
            StateSnap s{};
            if (!ReadSnap(s)) { fprintf(stderr, "SHM read failed\n"); return 1; }
            PrintStatePlain(s);
            return 0;
        }
        if (!_stricmp(argv[i], "--unload") || !_stricmp(argv[i], "-u")) {
            if (!g_stopEvent) { fprintf(stderr, "Stop event not opened\n"); return 1; }
            SetEvent(g_stopEvent);
            if (g_doneEvent) WaitForSingleObject(g_doneEvent, 2000);
            else             Sleep(500);
            printf("Stop signal sent.\n");
            return 0;
        }
        if (!_stricmp(argv[i], "--help") || !_stricmp(argv[i], "-h") ||
            !_stricmp(argv[i], "/?"))
        {
            printf("CS2 IsValveDS Spoofer console v1.14\n"
                   "  (no args)         interactive TUI\n"
                   "  --freeze-on VAL   enable freeze, value=0|1, exit\n"
                   "  --freeze-off      clear freeze, exit\n"
                   "  --set-value VAL   one-shot write, exit\n"
                   "  --print-state     dump SHM as text, exit\n"
                   "  --unload / -u     tell driver to stop, exit\n");
            return 0;
        }
    }
    return -1;
}

// --- Fatal / Ctrl-C / main --------------------------------------------------
static void Fatal(const char* fmt, ...)
{
    va_list ap; va_start(ap, fmt);
    printf(R_BR_RED "[FATAL] ");
    vprintf(fmt, ap);
    printf(R_RESET "\n");
    va_end(ap);
    printf("Press any key to exit.\n");
    fflush(stdout);
    _getch();
}

static BOOL WINAPI CtrlHandler(DWORD ctrl)
{
    if (ctrl == CTRL_C_EVENT || ctrl == CTRL_BREAK_EVENT ||
        ctrl == CTRL_CLOSE_EVENT)
    {
        g_quit.store(true);
        return TRUE;
    }
    return FALSE;
}

int main(int argc, char** argv)
{
    EnableVT();
    SetConsoleCtrlHandler(CtrlHandler, TRUE);

    // --- Open SHM ------------------------------------------------------------
    g_shmHandle = OpenFileMappingA(FILE_MAP_ALL_ACCESS, FALSE,
                                    ISVALVEDS_SHM_USER_NAME);
    if (!g_shmHandle) {
        DWORD e = GetLastError();
        Fatal("Cannot open SHM %s (err %lu). Load the driver first (run.bat / kdmapper).",
              ISVALVEDS_SHM_USER_NAME, e);
        return 1;
    }
    g_state = (ISVALVEDS_STATE*)MapViewOfFile(g_shmHandle, FILE_MAP_ALL_ACCESS,
                                               0, 0, sizeof(ISVALVEDS_STATE));
    if (!g_state) {
        DWORD e = GetLastError();
        Fatal("MapViewOfFile failed (err %lu).", e);
        CloseHandle(g_shmHandle);
        return 1;
    }

    // Wait briefly for the driver's first publish (magic set).
    {
        DWORD start = GetTickCount();
        bool  ok    = false;
        while (GetTickCount() - start < 3000) {
            if (g_state->magic == ISVALVEDS_MAGIC) { ok = true; break; }
            Sleep(50);
        }
        if (!ok) {
            Fatal("SHM magic never became valid (0x%08X != 0x%08X). Driver alive?",
                  g_state->magic, ISVALVEDS_MAGIC);
            UnmapViewOfFile(g_state);
            CloseHandle(g_shmHandle);
            return 1;
        }
        if (g_state->protocol_version != 2) {
            Fatal("Protocol mismatch: driver=v%u, console=v2. Rebuild the driver.",
                  g_state->protocol_version);
            UnmapViewOfFile(g_state);
            CloseHandle(g_shmHandle);
            return 1;
        }
    }

    g_stopEvent = OpenEventA(EVENT_MODIFY_STATE, FALSE, ISVALVEDS_STOP_USER_NAME);
    g_doneEvent = OpenEventA(SYNCHRONIZE,        FALSE, ISVALVEDS_DONE_USER_NAME);

    // --- CLI mode (skip TUI if any recognized flag) --------------------------
    if (argc > 1) {
        int rc = CliMode(argc, argv);
        if (rc >= 0) {
            if (g_state)     UnmapViewOfFile(g_state);
            if (g_shmHandle) CloseHandle(g_shmHandle);
            if (g_stopEvent) CloseHandle(g_stopEvent);
            if (g_doneEvent) CloseHandle(g_doneEvent);
            return rc;
        }
    }

    // --- Enter TUI -----------------------------------------------------------
    g_autoThread = std::thread(AutoToggleThread);
    printf(VT_CLEAR_ALL);
    SetStatus(R_GRAY, "Connected. Protocol v%u.", g_state->protocol_version);

    StateSnap lastSnap{};
    while (!g_quit.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)) {
        StateSnap s{};
        ReadSnap(s); // may leave s.valid=false; renderer handles that
        lastSnap = s;
        Render(s);

        int hz = g_refreshHz.load();
        if (hz < 1) hz = 1;
        DWORD frameMs = (DWORD)(1000 / hz);
        if (frameMs < 33) frameMs = 33;

        DWORD start = GetTickCount();
        while (GetTickCount() - start < frameMs) {
            if (_kbhit()) {
                int c = _getch();
                HandleKey(c, lastSnap);
                break; // re-render immediately after any key
            }
            Sleep(10);
        }
    }

    // --- Teardown ------------------------------------------------------------
    AutoToggleStop();
    if (g_autoThread.joinable()) g_autoThread.join();

    printf(R_RESET VT_SHOW VT_CLEAR_ALL);
    printf("Console exited. Driver still running (use --unload or [U] to stop it).\n");
    fflush(stdout);

    if (g_state)     UnmapViewOfFile(g_state);
    if (g_shmHandle) CloseHandle(g_shmHandle);
    if (g_stopEvent) CloseHandle(g_stopEvent);
    if (g_doneEvent) CloseHandle(g_doneEvent);
    return 0;
}

```

`source/apps/CS2IsValveDSSpooferConsole/shared.h`:

```h
#pragma once

//
// Shared definitions between kernel driver and user-mode console.
// Keep identical copies in IsValveDS_Driver/ and IsValveDS_Console/.
//
// Architecture (F20Driver-style, kdmapper-safe):
//   - NO IRP / IoCreateDevice. kdmapper-mapped drivers should not register
//     IRP dispatch because their DRIVER_OBJECT pointer goes stale once
//     DriverEntry returns.
//   - Driver creates a named SECTION (shared memory) and a named EVENT.
//   - User mode opens both by name (OpenFileMapping + MapViewOfFile,
//     OpenEvent + SetEvent).
//

// Kernel-side names: create the objects directly under \BaseNamedObjects,
// NOT under \BaseNamedObjects\Global. The user-mode "Global\X" prefix is a
// Win32 shorthand that resolves to \BaseNamedObjects\X (without an extra
// "Global" path component). On stock Windows there is also a symlink
// \BaseNamedObjects\Global -> \BaseNamedObjects, which is why the old paths
// happened to work, but on hardened / modified Windows builds (one user
// reported "Windows 10 22H2 ProMod UEFI") that symlink may not be present,
// and the user-mode console would silently fail to open SHM. Matching what
// F20Driver already does keeps both kits behavior-consistent.
#define ISVALVEDS_SHM_KERNEL_NAME    L"\\BaseNamedObjects\\IsValveDSState"
#define ISVALVEDS_STOP_KERNEL_NAME   L"\\BaseNamedObjects\\IsValveDSStop"
#define ISVALVEDS_DONE_KERNEL_NAME   L"\\BaseNamedObjects\\IsValveDSStopped"

// User-mode names (Win32 layer translates "Global\X" -> \BaseNamedObjects\X).
#define ISVALVEDS_SHM_USER_NAME      "Global\\IsValveDSState"
#define ISVALVEDS_STOP_USER_NAME     "Global\\IsValveDSStop"
#define ISVALVEDS_DONE_USER_NAME     "Global\\IsValveDSStopped"

// Sanity marker; driver writes this *last* after updating current_* fields.
// Consumer must verify magic==ISVALVEDS_MAGIC after reading.
#define ISVALVEDS_MAGIC              0x1DEA1D5Cu

// Error codes shared by current_error and write_error.
#define VDS_ERR_OK                   0
#define VDS_ERR_NO_CS2               1
#define VDS_ERR_NO_CLIENT_DLL        2
#define VDS_ERR_GAMERULES_NULL       3
#define VDS_ERR_READ_FAILED          4
#define VDS_ERR_WRITE_FAILED         5
#define VDS_ERR_BAD_VALUE            6
#define VDS_ERR_TIMEOUT              7

#pragma pack(push, 1)
typedef struct _ISVALVEDS_STATE {
    unsigned int       magic;               // = ISVALVEDS_MAGIC when current_* valid

    // ---- driver-owned (user reads, never writes) ------------------------
    int                current_value;        // 0 / 1, or -1 if unavailable
    unsigned int       current_error;        // VDS_ERR_*
    unsigned long long current_address;      // abs addr of m_bIsValveDS in cs2
    unsigned long long last_poll_systime;    // KeQuerySystemTime() snapshot
    unsigned int       driver_tick;          // monotonically increasing per poll
    unsigned int       cs2_pid;              // 0 if cs2 not found
    unsigned long long client_base;          // client.dll base in cs2 (or 0)

    // ---- user-owned (driver reads, never writes) ------------------------
    unsigned int       desired_value;        // 0 or 1
    unsigned int       write_request_id;     // user increments to request write

    // ---- driver-owned write response (user reads) -----------------------
    unsigned int       write_handled_id;     // matches write_request_id when done
    unsigned int       write_error;          // VDS_ERR_* for the last write
    int                write_result_value;   // value after the last write attempt
    unsigned long long write_handled_systime;// KeQuerySystemTime at completion

    // ==================== v2 APPEND ONLY BELOW ====================
    // Freeze mode: when enabled, driver unconditionally re-writes freeze_value
    // every worker iteration (200ms), IGNORING write_request_id ping-pong.
    // Semantics: freeze_enabled OVERRIDES write_request_id. To disable freeze
    // and go back to one-shot writes, set freeze_enabled=0.
    unsigned int       freeze_enabled;       // 0/1 - user writes
    unsigned int       freeze_value;         // 0/1 - user writes
    unsigned long long freeze_writes;        // driver-owned counter of re-applies
    unsigned int       freeze_errors;        // driver-owned MmCopy failure count during freeze
    unsigned int       protocol_version;     // = 2

    unsigned int       pad[10];
} ISVALVEDS_STATE;
#pragma pack(pop)

```

`source/apps/CS2RankSpooferConsole/README.md`:

```md
# CS2RankSpooferConsole

**[EN](#english) · [RU / Русский](#русский)**

---

## English

User-mode console for managing `CS2RankSpooferDriver.sys`. Allows setting rank/wins/losses values for three CS2 modes: Premier (mode 11), Wingman (mode 7), Competitive (mode 1).

### Version-independence

The console works through the driver's SHM API — client.dll offsets are not used. The driver itself finds `get_rank_data` via pattern scan.

### Build

```powershell
& "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community\MSBuild\Current\Bin\MSBuild.exe" `
  source\apps\CS2RankSpooferConsole\CS2RankSpooferConsole.vcxproj `
  /p:Configuration=Release /p:Platform=x64
```

Output: `x64\Release\CS2RankSpooferConsole.exe` (~220 KB).

### Ranges

- **Premier**: CS rating 0..35000
- **Competitive** / **Wingman**: rank 0..18

### Usage

The console writes `RANKSPOOF_MODE` (rank/wins/losses/enabled) into SHM for the three modes. The driver copies them into a resident page of cs2 each tick, and the trampoline shellcode rewrites out-params on every call to `get_rank_data`.

### Named objects (shared with driver)

| Type | Name (Win32) | Kernel path |
|---|---|---|
| SECTION | `Global\CS2RankSpoofState` | `\BaseNamedObjects\CS2RankSpoofState` |
| EVENT | `Global\CS2RankSpoofStop` | `\BaseNamedObjects\CS2RankSpoofStop` |
| EVENT | `Global\CS2RankSpoofStopped` | `\BaseNamedObjects\CS2RankSpoofStopped` |

All definitions in `shared.h`. Magic: `RANKSPOOF_MAGIC = 0x52415358 ('RASX')`.

---

## Русский

User-mode консоль для управления `CS2RankSpooferDriver.sys`. Позволяет задавать значения rank/wins/losses для трёх режимов CS2: Premier (mode 11), Wingman (mode 7), Competitive (mode 1).

### Version-независимость

Консоль работает через SHM API драйвера — client.dll оффсеты не используются. Драйвер сам находит `get_rank_data` через pattern scan.

### Сборка

```powershell
& "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community\MSBuild\Current\Bin\MSBuild.exe" `
  source\apps\CS2RankSpooferConsole\CS2RankSpooferConsole.vcxproj `
  /p:Configuration=Release /p:Platform=x64
```

Выход: `x64\Release\CS2RankSpooferConsole.exe` (~220 KB).

### Ranges

- **Premier**: CS rating 0..35000
- **Competitive** / **Wingman**: rank 0..18

### Использование

Консоль пишет `RANKSPOOF_MODE` (rank/wins/losses/enabled) в SHM для трёх режимов. Драйвер копирует их в резидентную страницу cs2 каждый тик, а trampoline shellcode переписывает out-params при каждом вызове `get_rank_data`.

### Named objects (общие с драйвером)

| Тип | Имя (Win32) | Kernel-путь |
|---|---|---|
| SECTION | `Global\CS2RankSpoofState` | `\BaseNamedObjects\CS2RankSpoofState` |
| EVENT | `Global\CS2RankSpoofStop` | `\BaseNamedObjects\CS2RankSpoofStop` |
| EVENT | `Global\CS2RankSpoofStopped` | `\BaseNamedObjects\CS2RankSpoofStopped` |

Все definitions в `shared.h`. Magic: `RANKSPOOF_MAGIC = 0x52415358 ('RASX')`.
