`source/dlls/SafetyPlugin_recovered/core/dllmain.pseudo.cpp`:

```cpp
// ============================================================================
// dllmain.pseudo.cpp   FVA entry / TLS callback / OEP
//
// Recovered from rebuild.i64 decompilation; not verified to compile.
// The DllMain and TLS_Callback bodies are VMP-VIRTUALIZED — only I/O
// boundaries and control-flow anchors are known. Everything below is a
// structural skeleton, NOT a byte-for-byte port.
//
// Anchors (RVA vs image base 0x7FFBE8000000):
//   OEP                          0x122C     (Scylla fix-dump override)
//   Packer entry                 0x3ED6D9
//   TLS_Callback_0               0x374254
//   Shared VMP dispatcher        sub_7FFF424EE1C0   (RVA 0x43E1C0)
//   Tail-return handler          sub_7FFF424D44B7   (RVA 0x4244B7)
//                                  pop r10 ; add r10, -0x200EE ; jmp r10
//   IAT NtProtectVirtualMemory   RVA 0x23B020
//
// Cross-ref: VLB dllmain.cpp   (constructs the hook installer graph; not a
//                               direct port of the FVA entry.)
// ============================================================================

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// ORIGINAL DECOMPILED BODY (excerpt; VMP-BLOCKED)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
/*
; RVA 0x122C  (OEP)
sub_OEP proc near
    ; VMEntry pattern (fingerprintable, VMP 3.6-3.8):
    ;   push    rbx
    ;   movabs  rbx, imm64        ; VM context
    ;   mov     dword ptr [rsp+8], imm32
    ;   mov     rbx, [rsp]
    ;   call    sub_VMEntry_body
    ; -> control funnels into shared dispatcher sub_7FFF424EE1C0.
    ; Any 64-byte window that also contains NtQueryInformationProcess or
    ; NtSetInformationThread indicates VMP >= 3.6 (extra AntiDebug layer).
sub_OEP endp

; RVA 0x374254  (TLS_Callback_0)
TLS_Callback_0 proc near
    ; VMP-obfuscated; observably touches PEB->BeingDebugged, NtGlobalFlag,
    ; and dispatches through sub_7FFF424EE1C0. First fire is BEFORE OEP,
    ; which is why the debug plan sets bpdll BEFORE inject.
TLS_Callback_0 endp

; Packer entry 0x3ED6D9  -> must fire at least once, then wait for 8 s of
; zero-hits on NtProtectVirtualMemory IAT slot (RVA 0x23B020) as the
; "settled" signal for Scylla dump.
*/

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// TYPE-ANNOTATED PSEUDO-C SKELETON (structural only)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

#include <cstdint>
#include <windows.h>   // for DllMain reason codes and PIMAGE_TLS_CALLBACK

// FVA installer surfaces (forward decls; bodies live in the hooks/ files).
extern "C" bool fva_install_hook_a_create_move();          // H1
extern "C" bool fva_install_hook_b_level_init();           // H2
extern "C" bool fva_install_hook_c_serialize_to_array();   // H3
extern "C" bool fva_uninstall_all_hooks();

// Anti-debug plumbing (VMP-BLOCKED body; only known-good stub here).
extern "C" void fva_anti_debug_bringup();
extern "C" void fva_anti_debug_teardown();

// Dynamic import resolver (sub_7FFBE82ED25E, 1.7 MB, VMP).
// This is the ONLY site in the corpus that references SetThreadContext /
// SuspendThread / Thread32First / Thread32Next. All indirect IAT resolution
// funnels through it. Not decompilable without full VMP unwind.
extern "C" void* fva_resolve_import(const char* dll, const char* name);

// ---- TLS_Callback_0  (RVA 0x374254) --------------------------------------
extern "C" VOID NTAPI FVA_TlsCallback0(PVOID DllHandle,
                                       DWORD Reason,
                                       PVOID Reserved) {
    (void)DllHandle; (void)Reserved;
    // VMP-BLOCKED: real body probes PEB debug flags, sets up anti-attach,
    // and chains through the shared dispatcher sub_7FFF424EE1C0.
    switch (Reason) {
        case DLL_PROCESS_ATTACH:
            fva_anti_debug_bringup();
            break;
        case DLL_PROCESS_DETACH:
            fva_anti_debug_teardown();
            break;
        default:
            break;
    }
}

// ---- DllMain (OEP RVA 0x122C) --------------------------------------------
extern "C" BOOL WINAPI DllMain(HINSTANCE hInstance,
                               DWORD     fdwReason,
                               LPVOID    lpvReserved) {
    (void)hInstance; (void)lpvReserved;
    switch (fdwReason) {
        case DLL_PROCESS_ATTACH: {
            // Install the three CONFIRMED hooks. In FVA the actual install
            // path goes through the VMP dispatcher and uses HW BPs / kernel
            // shadowing (see km_driver.c) because the 5.6 kHz
            // NtReadVirtualMemory self-scan defeats user-mode inline hooks.
            if (!fva_install_hook_a_create_move())        return FALSE;
            if (!fva_install_hook_b_level_init())         return FALSE;
            if (!fva_install_hook_c_serialize_to_array()) return FALSE;
            break;
        }
        case DLL_PROCESS_DETACH:
            fva_uninstall_all_hooks();
            break;
        default:
            break;
    }
    return TRUE;
}

// ===========================================================================
// VMP TAIL-RETURN CANARY
//
// sub_7FFF424D44B7  (RVA 0x4244B7):
//     pop     r10
//     add     r10, -0x200EE
//     jmp     r10
// Every VMP handler that "returns to the caller" reaches this stub. When
// tracing, hitting 0x4244B7 with a plausible r10 value is a signal that
// the previous handler is done; use it as a chunking boundary in the
// Colby57 p-code-to-p-code recompile.
// ===========================================================================

```

`source/dlls/SafetyPlugin_recovered/core/gate_check.pseudo.cpp`:

```cpp
// ============================================================================
// gate_check.pseudo.cpp   byte_7FFBE823A9A0 init + check
//
// Recovered from rebuild.i64 decompilation; not verified to compile.
// The gate byte lives at RVA 0x23A9A0 (relative to FVA image base
// 0x7FFBE8000000). It is an ENABLE flag: non-zero => Section-J angle /
// input_history reconstruction runs; zero => the ~900-byte reconstruction
// block is skipped via JZ to loc_7FFBE80CAA2F.
//
// Init site:   0x7FFBE80CA00B  (inside sub_7FFBE80C9D8C = Hook A body)
// Check site:  0x7FFBE80CA6B4  (same function, ~1.7 KB later)
// Cross-ref:   docs/gate_byte_logic.md   (full semantics)
//              VLB create_move_hook.cpp   (VLB uses an equivalent internal
//              gate exposed as FVA_GATE_MODE_BYTE / FVA_GATE_MODE_ATTACK)
// ============================================================================

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// ORIGINAL DECOMPILED / DISASSEMBLED SITES
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
/*
; --- 0x7FFBE80CA00B  init ------------------------------------------------
    ; (TLS-once guard elided by IDA)
    call    sub_7FFBE80FB1C4        ; FNV1a decrypt of dword_7FFBE823A9CC
                                    ; with key 0xB6E8F068409FEF6C
    ; rax = decoded RVA-cache table base
    mov     cl, byte ptr [rax+rdi]  ; rdi = index into the manifest
    mov     cs:byte_7FFBE823A9A0, cl
    mov     [rax+rbx], dil          ; dil == 0 -> zero companion slot at rbx

; --- 0x7FFBE80CA6B4  check -----------------------------------------------
    xor     r13d, r13d              ; (from 0x7FFBE80CA096; r13b = 0)
    ; ...
    cmp     cs:byte_7FFBE823A9A0, r13b
    jz      loc_7FFBE80CAA2F        ; skip ~900 B of Section-J
    ; fallthrough -> 0x7FFBE80CA6C1 Section-J body
    ; (see hooks/create_move_hook.pseudo.cpp and
    ;      core/input_history_spoof.pseudo.cpp)
*/

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// TYPE-ANNOTATED PSEUDO-C SKELETON
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

#include <cstdint>

// Storage at RVA 0x23A9A0.
extern "C" uint8_t g_gate_byte;                       // byte_7FFBE823A9A0
extern "C" uint32_t g_rva_cache_encoded;              // dword_7FFBE823A9CC
static constexpr uint64_t kRvaCacheKey = 0xB6E8F068409FEF6Cull;

extern "C" uint64_t fva_fnv1a_decrypt(uint32_t enc, uint64_t key);  // sub_7FFBE80FB1C4

// ---- Init  (0x7FFBE80CA00B) ----------------------------------------------
// Called ONCE per process on the first traversal of Hook A body. TLS-once
// guarded in the original. Populates the gate byte from a decoded manifest
// entry, then zeros a companion slot.
extern "C" void fva_gate_byte_init(size_t manifest_index, size_t companion_index) {
    uint8_t* table = reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(
        fva_fnv1a_decrypt(g_rva_cache_encoded, kRvaCacheKey));
    g_gate_byte             = table[manifest_index];
    table[companion_index]  = 0;
}

// ---- Check (0x7FFBE80CA6B4) ----------------------------------------------
// Returns true when Section-J must run. External tooling can force the state
// by writing g_gate_byte directly — no other predicate participates.
extern "C" inline bool fva_gate_is_armed() {
    return g_gate_byte != 0;
}

// ---- External arm/disarm helpers -----------------------------------------
// Per the FVA memory note ("Section-J автономен, gate только
// byte_7FFBE823A9A0 != 0; g_target_armed в reconstruction = ошибка,
// self-echo делать через delta=0 БЕЗ append") the correct external control
// path is these two helpers. Do NOT reintroduce a g_target_armed variable
// in reconstruction code and do NOT append a self-echo entry — issue a
// delta=0 write into the existing entry instead.
extern "C" void fva_gate_arm()   { g_gate_byte = 1; }
extern "C" void fva_gate_disarm(){ g_gate_byte = 0; }

// ===========================================================================
// CROSS-CHECK NOTES  (see docs/gate_byte_logic.md)
//
// - Polarity: non_zero_enables. r13b at the check site is 0 (xor r13d,r13d
//   at 0x7FFBE80CA096); `jz` = jump if equal = jump if byte == 0 = skip.
// - Skip target: loc_7FFBE80CAA2F  (~900 B past the check).
// - Init source: manifest table decrypted via FNV1a (key
//   0xB6E8F068409FEF6C) from dword_7FFBE823A9CC.
// - Only site that WRITES the byte from FVA code is 0x7FFBE80CA00B.
//   External writes are the intended arm/disarm mechanism.
// ===========================================================================

```

`source/dlls/SafetyPlugin_recovered/core/input_history_spoof.pseudo.cpp`:

```cpp
// ============================================================================
// input_history_spoof.pseudo.cpp   Section-J reconstruction
//
// Recovered from rebuild.i64 decompilation; not verified to compile.
// Section-J is the ~900-byte block inside sub_7FFBE80C9D8C starting at
// 0x7FFBE80CA6C1 that runs when the gate byte at RVA 0x23A9A0 is non-zero.
// It is the ONLY code path in FVA that mutates CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB
// entries on the outgoing user-cmd; it does NOT touch subtick_moves.
//
// Cross-ref:
//   core/gate_check.pseudo.cpp                      the gate byte
//   hooks/create_move_hook.pseudo.cpp               the Hook A body host
//   docs/protobuf_schema.md                         CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB shape
//   VLB input_history_spoof.cpp                     equivalent VLB path
// ============================================================================

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// ORIGINAL DECOMPILED BODY (disasm-level; Hex-Rays is patchy here because
// of the surrounding VMP context switches.)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
/*
; entry: 0x7FFBE80CA6C1  (falls through when byte_7FFBE823A9A0 != 0)

    mov     rbx, cs:qword_7FFBE823C250      ; QAngle* reference (view / target)
    mov     rsi, cs:qword_7FFBE823A918      ; CSGOUserCmdPB* user-cmd ctx
    or      dword ptr [rsi+20h], r15d       ; set a PB flag bit in ctx+0x20
    mov     rdi, [rsi+40h]                  ; CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB* head
    test    rdi, rdi
    jnz     have_entry
    ; ---- lazy allocate an input_history entry --------------------------
    call    sub_7FFBE80FAD0C                ; returns new entry ptr
    mov     rdi, rax
have_entry:
    mov     rcx, [rdi+40h]                  ; sub-object slot (view_angles owner)
    call    sub_7FFBE80F6FEC                ; deref helper -> rdi (adjusted)
    ; ---- read current view_angles on the entry --------------------------
    movss   xmm0, dword ptr [rdi+18h]       ; pitch
    movss   xmm1, dword ptr [rdi+1Ch]       ; yaw
    movss   xmm2, dword ptr [rdi+20h]       ; roll
    ; ---- delta against reference ----------------------------------------
    subss   xmm0, dword ptr [rbx]
    subss   xmm1, dword ptr [rbx+4]
    subss   xmm2, dword ptr [rbx+8]
    ; ---- per-axis wrap into [-180, 180) ---------------------------------
    ;   const_180  = dword_7FFBE82190E8  (=  180.0f)
    ;   const_360  = dword_7FFBE82190EC  (=  360.0f)
    ;   const_-180 = dword_7FFBE82190F4  (= -180.0f)
    ; per axis: sub, vcomiss, vaddss ... via sub_7FFBE81B3D60 (fmod-like)
    ; ---- write back -----------------------------------------------------
    movss   dword ptr [rdi+18h], xmm0
    movss   dword ptr [rdi+1Ch], xmm1
    movss   dword ptr [rdi+20h], xmm2

    ; ... additional per-frame bookkeeping omitted (subtick_moves is NOT
    ; touched here; per memory `fva-targets-input-history`, FVA targets
    ; CSGOUserCmdPB.input_history through vtable RVA 0x1A1E028, not
    ; CSubtickMoveStep.)
*/

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// TYPE-ANNOTATED PSEUDO-C SKELETON
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef>

struct QAngle { float pitch, yaw, roll; };

// Opaque protobuf ctx and entry (see docs/protobuf_schema.md).
struct CSGOUserCmdPB;
struct CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB;   // CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB::New @ RVA 0x742730, size 120

// FVA globals
extern "C" uint8_t         g_gate_byte;                    // byte_7FFBE823A9A0
extern "C" QAngle*         g_reference_angles;             // qword_7FFBE823C250
extern "C" CSGOUserCmdPB*  g_user_cmd_ctx;                 // qword_7FFBE823A918

// Constant view (rebuild image base 0x7FFBE8000000; RVAs preserved).
static constexpr float kAngleWrap180  =  180.0f;   // dword_7FFBE82190E8
static constexpr float kAngleWrap360  =  360.0f;   // dword_7FFBE82190EC
static constexpr float kAngleWrapN180 = -180.0f;   // dword_7FFBE82190F4

// FVA helpers
extern "C" CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB* fva_lazy_alloc_input_history_entry(CSGOUserCmdPB*); // sub_7FFBE80FAD0C
extern "C" void*                    fva_input_entry_subobject(CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB*);// sub_7FFBE80F6FEC
extern "C" float                    fva_fmod_like_wrap(float x, float mod);             // sub_7FFBE81B3D60

// ---- wrap a signed angle into [-180, 180) --------------------------------
static inline float fva_wrap_angle(float x) {
    // Equivalent to  fmodf(x + 180, 360) - 180  with sign handling that
    // matches sub_7FFBE81B3D60 (see sub-pattern in the disasm above).
    return fva_fmod_like_wrap(x + kAngleWrap180, kAngleWrap360) + kAngleWrapN180;
}

// ---- Section-J entry point (called when g_gate_byte != 0) ----------------
extern "C" void fva_section_j_input_history_reconstruct() {
    if (!g_gate_byte) return;   // idempotent guard mirroring the JZ

    CSGOUserCmdPB* ctx = g_user_cmd_ctx;
    // PB flag bit at ctx+0x20 (r15d source in the original; treated as a
    // known-1 bit in reconstruction contexts).
    *reinterpret_cast<uint32_t*>(reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(ctx) + 0x20) |= 1u;

    // Head of input_history at ctx+0x40 (per usercmd.pb MergeFrom line 507,
    // sub_7FFBE80F853C: 3 qword ptr fields at +0x18 / +0x20 / +0x28 on the
    // outer message; the FVA context struct at qword_7FFBE823A918 mirrors a
    // subset of that layout with the input_history head hoisted to +0x40).
    CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB* entry =
        *reinterpret_cast<CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB**>(
            reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(ctx) + 0x40);
    if (!entry) {
        entry = fva_lazy_alloc_input_history_entry(ctx);
    }

    // View-angle sub-object owner at entry+0x40; deref helper adjusts to the
    // actual QAngle slot inside the sub-message.
    QAngle* view = reinterpret_cast<QAngle*>(
        static_cast<uint8_t*>(fva_input_entry_subobject(entry)) + 0x18);

    // NOTE (memory: fva-no-hotkey):
    //   "self-echo делать через delta=0 БЕЗ append"
    // -> when we are echoing to keep parity, apply a zero-delta write into
    //    the EXISTING entry rather than allocating and appending a new one.
    const QAngle delta = {
        view->pitch - g_reference_angles->pitch,
        view->yaw   - g_reference_angles->yaw,
        view->roll  - g_reference_angles->roll,
    };
    view->pitch = fva_wrap_angle(delta.pitch);
    view->yaw   = fva_wrap_angle(delta.yaw);
    view->roll  = fva_wrap_angle(delta.roll);
}

// ===========================================================================
// CROSS-CHECK NOTES  (see docs/vlb_gap.md and docs/protobuf_schema.md)
//
// - VLB path performs the same delta+wrap on view_angles(field=2) of the
//   CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB (proto tag 2, CMsgQAngle). VLB wraps the block
//   in engine2_stable + FVA_GATE_MODE_BYTE, while FVA relies solely on
//   byte_7FFBE823A9A0.
// - subtick_moves (field 18 on CBaseUserCmdPB) is NOT touched by Section-J;
//   any VLB code that mutates subtick_moves belongs elsewhere and is a
//   parity divergence — flag it in docs/vlb_gap.md.
// - Entry factory: CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB::New @ RVA 0x742730 (120 B).
//   Signature `B9 78` is not unique — pin the address via RTTI /
//   vtable-pointer table (see memory: fva-targets-input-history and
//   cs2-t-new-rva-hardcoded).
// - byte 4 of the ctor sled is **0x10**, not 0x08 — flagged in the
//   cs2-t-new-rva-hardcoded memory as a common porting mistake.
// ===========================================================================

```

`source/dlls/SafetyPlugin_recovered/docs/gate_byte_logic.md`:

```md
# Gate byte logic: `byte_7FFBE823A9A0`

**[EN](#english) · [RU / Русский](#русский)**

---

## English

### Summary

`byte_7FFBE823A9A0` is an **ENABLE** flag (armed = non-zero). Semantics:

- **byte == 0** => `JZ` taken at `0x7FFBE80CA6B4` => execution jumps over ~900 B of Section-J code to `loc_7FFBE80CAA2F`.
- **byte != 0** => fall through into the Section-J input_history / angle-fixup path.

This exactly matches the memory note `fva-no-hotkey`:

> "Section-J автономен, gate только `byte_7FFBE823A9A0 != 0`; `g_target_armed` в reconstruction = ошибка, self-echo делать через delta=0 БЕЗ append."

No hotkey, no `g_target_armed`, only this single byte gates whether the reconstruction runs.

### Check site (0x7FFBE80CA6B4)

```
cmp     cs:byte_7FFBE823A9A0, r13b     ; r13b = 0 (xor r13d, r13d @ 0x7FFBE80CA096)
jz      loc_7FFBE80CAA2F               ; skip ~900 B of Section-J
```

### Reconstruction block gated behind the non-zero byte (0x7FFBE80CA6C1)

- loads `qword_7FFBE823C250` (view-angles / reference-vector source) into `rbx`
- loads `qword_7FFBE823A918` (CSGOUserCmdPB / input-state ctx) into `rsi`
- ORs an `r15d` bit into `[rsi+0x20]` (a PB flag word)
- pulls `[rsi+0x40]` as an input_history entry pointer, lazy-allocates via `sub_7FFBE80FAD0C` if null
- dereferences an inner sub-object at `[rdi+0x40]` via `sub_7FFBE80F6FEC`
- loads three floats at `+0x18` / `+0x1C` / `+0x20` (view_angles.pitch / yaw / roll on the entry)
- computes deltas against `[rbx]`, `[rbx+4]`, `[rbx+8]`
- normalizes each into `[-180, 180)` using the sub / vcomiss / vaddss pattern against constants
    - `dword_7FFBE82190E8 = 180.0f`
    - `dword_7FFBE82190EC = 360.0f`
    - `dword_7FFBE82190F4 = -180.0f`
  with `sub_7FFBE81B3D60` as the fmod-like helper.

Net effect: the gate specifically controls the **view-angle patch step** of the reconstructed `CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB`.

### Init site (0x7FFBE80CA00B)

The single write to `byte_7FFBE823A9A0` from FVA code is at `0x7FFBE80CA00B` and is unconditional relative to the gate itself:

```
; (TLS-once guard)
call    sub_7FFBE80FB1C4               ; FNV1a decrypt of dword_7FFBE823A9CC
                                       ; with key 0xB6E8F068409FEF6C
mov     cl, [rax+rdi]                  ; rax = decoded RVA table base, rdi = index
mov     cs:byte_7FFBE823A9A0, cl       ; <-- populate gate byte from manifest
mov     [rax+rbx], dil                 ; dil == 0 -> zero companion slot at rbx
```

So the gate byte is populated from a manifest/config byte at first traversal of `sub_7FFBE80C9D8C`, then subsequently used at `0x7FFBE80CA6B4` to arm or disarm the angle-reconstruction path.

### External control

- **Force-enable** Section-J angle patching: write a non-zero value directly into `byte_7FFBE823A9A0` after DLL init.
- **Force-disable**: clear the byte.
- No other predicate participates in the gate.

### Structured record

```json
{
  "gate_flag": {
    "addr":                    "0x7FFBE823A9A0",
    "name":                    "byte_7FFBE823A9A0",
    "role":                    "gate_enable_flag",
    "polarity":                "non_zero_enables",
    "gate_site":               "0x7FFBE80CA6B4",
    "init_site":               "0x7FFBE80CA00B",
    "controlling_function":    "sub_7FFBE80C9D8C",
    "notes":                   "cmp byte, r13b(=0); jz skips reconstruction block. Non-zero -> Section-J angle/input_history reconstruction runs."
  },
  "gate_site": {
    "addr":                    "0x7FFBE80CA6B4",
    "insn":                    "cmp cs:byte_7FFBE823A9A0, r13b ; jz loc_7FFBE80CAA2F",
    "skip_target":             "loc_7FFBE80CAA2F",
    "reconstruction_entry":    "0x7FFBE80CA6C1"
  }
}
```

---

## Русский

### Сводка

`byte_7FFBE823A9A0` — **ENABLE** флаг (armed = non-zero). Семантика:

- **byte == 0** => `JZ` taken на `0x7FFBE80CA6B4` => исполнение перепрыгивает через ~900 Б кода Section-J на `loc_7FFBE80CAA2F`.
- **byte != 0** => падает вниз в путь Section-J input_history / angle-fixup.

Это точно совпадает с memory-заметкой `fva-no-hotkey`:

> «Section-J автономен, gate только `byte_7FFBE823A9A0 != 0`; `g_target_armed` в reconstruction = ошибка, self-echo делать через delta=0 БЕЗ append.»

Никакой хоткей, никакой `g_target_armed`, только этот один байт гейтит запуск reconstruction'а.

### Check site (0x7FFBE80CA6B4)

```
cmp     cs:byte_7FFBE823A9A0, r13b     ; r13b = 0 (xor r13d, r13d @ 0x7FFBE80CA096)
jz      loc_7FFBE80CAA2F               ; skip ~900 B of Section-J
```

### Reconstruction-блок за non-zero байтом (0x7FFBE80CA6C1)

- загружает `qword_7FFBE823C250` (view-angles / reference-vector source) в `rbx`
- загружает `qword_7FFBE823A918` (CSGOUserCmdPB / input-state ctx) в `rsi`
- OR'ит бит `r15d` в `[rsi+0x20]` (PB flag word)
- достаёт `[rsi+0x40]` как указатель на input_history entry, lazy-allocates через `sub_7FFBE80FAD0C` если null
- разыменовывает внутренний под-объект на `[rdi+0x40]` через `sub_7FFBE80F6FEC`
- загружает три float'а на `+0x18` / `+0x1C` / `+0x20` (view_angles.pitch / yaw / roll на entry)
- вычисляет дельты против `[rbx]`, `[rbx+4]`, `[rbx+8]`
- нормализует каждую в `[-180, 180)` через sub / vcomiss / vaddss паттерн против констант
    - `dword_7FFBE82190E8 = 180.0f`
    - `dword_7FFBE82190EC = 360.0f`
    - `dword_7FFBE82190F4 = -180.0f`
  с `sub_7FFBE81B3D60` как fmod-like helper'ом.

Итоговый эффект: гейт конкретно управляет **шагом патчинга view-angle** реконструированного `CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB`.

### Init site (0x7FFBE80CA00B)

Единственная запись в `byte_7FFBE823A9A0` из FVA-кода находится на `0x7FFBE80CA00B` и она unconditional относительно самого гейта:

```
; (TLS-once guard)
call    sub_7FFBE80FB1C4               ; FNV1a-декрипт dword_7FFBE823A9CC
                                       ; с ключом 0xB6E8F068409FEF6C
mov     cl, [rax+rdi]                  ; rax = base decoded RVA table, rdi = индекс
mov     cs:byte_7FFBE823A9A0, cl       ; <-- заливаем гейт-байт из manifest'а
mov     [rax+rbx], dil                 ; dil == 0 -> зануляем companion-slot на rbx
```

Значит гейт-байт заливается из manifest/config байта на первом прохождении `sub_7FFBE80C9D8C`, потом далее используется на `0x7FFBE80CA6B4` для arm/disarm angle-reconstruction пути.

### Внешний контроль

- **Force-enable** Section-J angle patching: пишем non-zero значение напрямую в `byte_7FFBE823A9A0` после DLL init.
- **Force-disable**: обнуляем байт.
- Никакой другой предикат в гейте не участвует.

### Структурированная запись

```json
{
  "gate_flag": {
    "addr":                    "0x7FFBE823A9A0",
    "name":                    "byte_7FFBE823A9A0",
    "role":                    "gate_enable_flag",
    "polarity":                "non_zero_enables",
    "gate_site":               "0x7FFBE80CA6B4",
    "init_site":               "0x7FFBE80CA00B",
    "controlling_function":    "sub_7FFBE80C9D8C",
    "notes":                   "cmp byte, r13b(=0); jz skips reconstruction block. Non-zero -> Section-J angle/input_history reconstruction runs."
  },
  "gate_site": {
    "addr":                    "0x7FFBE80CA6B4",
    "insn":                    "cmp cs:byte_7FFBE823A9A0, r13b ; jz loc_7FFBE80CAA2F",
    "skip_target":             "loc_7FFBE80CAA2F",
    "reconstruction_entry":    "0x7FFBE80CA6C1"
  }
}
```

`source/dlls/SafetyPlugin_recovered/docs/playbook.md`:

```md
# FVA Devirtualization Playbook (consolidated)

**[EN](#english) · [RU / Русский](#русский)**

---

## English

Source: 16 deob_docs collapsed into a single 6-day plan.
Target image: `C:\vmp\FuckVacV2_vmprotect_dump_20260707_004535_pid59960_7FFF420B0000\FuckVacAgain_rebuild.exe`
Approach: p-code-to-p-code recompilation (Colby57 Triton + Z3), not a full lifter.

### Fixed anchors

| item                                | value                              |
|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| OEP RVA                             | 0x122C                              |
| Packer entry                        | 0x3ED6D9                            |
| TLS_Callback_0 RVA                  | 0x374254                            |
| Shared VMP dispatcher               | sub_7FFF424EE1C0 (RVA 0x43E1C0)     |
| Tail-return handler                 | sub_7FFF424D44B7 (RVA 0x4244B7)     |
| IAT NtProtectVirtualMemory          | RVA 0x23B020                        |
| VMP version                         | 3.6-3.8 (or 3.9.4 per one doc)      |

VMP version confirmable by checking whether AntiDebug executes past VMEntry.

### Prior-work ledger (from `FuckVacV2_dump.dll`)

- aaron-branded protobuf strings
- MyDriver23 kernel driver + `inject_ldrloaddll.ps1`
- `plugin_offsets.h` / `symbols.json`
- `implementation_theory/reconstruction/docs/FVA_CHECKLIST.md`
- `HOOK_A_DECOMP.md`

This plan advances the ledger from "ported from disasm" to "ported from pseudo-C".

### Blocking constraint

**5.6 kHz `NtReadVirtualMemory` self-scan on `.text`** kills user-mode inline hooks in seconds. Options:
- Kernel-mode shadow via `km_driver.c` lazy-init IOCTL surface, OR
- Hardware breakpoints only.

### Day plan

#### Day 1 — OEP capture + Scylla dump + VMEntry site scan

1. **OEP capture via mydbg stealth**
   `C:\vmp\dbg\mydbg(1).exe --stealth --attach-name cs2`
   INT3 / HW BP at `image_base + 0x122C`. Set deferred `bpdll FuckVacAgain.dll` BEFORE inject.
   Fallback settled-signal: 8 s of zero hits on NtProtectVirtualMemory IAT slot 0x23B020 AFTER packer entry 0x3ED6D9 has fired at least once.

2. **Scylla dump of unpacked FVA**
   Plugins -> Scylla in x64dbg (or `dump.module` MCP tool):
   - Pick `cs2.exe` process, pick `FuckVacAgain.dll` module.
   - IAT Autosearch; validate lands in `.=ly` (RVA 0x23B000, size 0x755).
   - Get Imports; cut red thunks.
   - Fix Dump with OEP override to 0x1228.
   - Save as `C:\vmp\fva_livecap\FuckVacAgain_scylla_unpacked.dll`.
   - Do NOT strip `.#I5` / `.#?n` / `.=ly` sections yet.

3. **VMEntry site scan** in IDA 8.4+ across `rebuild.exe.i64`:
   Pattern:
   `53 48 BB ?? ?? ?? ?? ?? ?? ?? ?? C7 44 24 08 ?? ?? ?? ?? 48 8B 1C 24 E8`
   (push rbx; movabs rbx, imm64; mov [rsp+8], imm32; mov rbx, [rsp]; call).
   Fingerprint VMP version: presence of `NtQueryInformationProcess` / `NtSetInformationThread` within 64 B past the call => VMP >= 3.6 (extra AntiDebug layer); absence => <= 3.5.

#### Day 2 — Handler taxonomy scan

Scan across `.#I5` and `.#?n` using the 10 canonical handler names:

- vEntry_Prologue
- vDispatcher_JmpR9
- vTapeFetch
- vValueCryptor_Chain
- vOperandDecrypt_XorDword
- vStateStore_MovStack
- vCPUID
- vBranch_Struct
- vExit_Epilogue_Oreans
- vCmp_Oreans_Stock

Also scan `FF 27` (jmp [rdi]) tail and `41 FF E1` (jmp r9) as parallel dispatcher variants. Output: `day2_handlers.json`.

#### Day 3 — Live trace

Inject FVA via `C:\Users\sshunko\...` PowerShell wrapper (`inject_ldrloaddll.ps1`). Collect Triton trace through the shared dispatcher `sub_7FFF424EE1C0` (17+ callers) with per-caller chunking on the tail-return canary at RVA 0x4244B7. Correlate with the Day 2 handler taxonomy.

#### Day 4 - 6

Not fully captured in the source summary (input truncated). Standard Colby57 continuation would be:
- Day 4: symbolic p-code emission per handler.
- Day 5: Z3-driven simplification + JIT-friendly reassembly of `.#I5` / `.#?n`.
- Day 6: parity diff against VLB + emit final pseudo-C to `SafetyPlugin_recovered/`.

### Warnings

- The 5.6 kHz self-scan requires kernel shadow or HW BPs; do not attempt user-mode inline hooks on FVA `.text`.
- The VMP dynamic import resolver `sub_7FFBE82ED25E` (1.7 MB) is the only site referencing `SetThreadContext` / `SuspendThread` / `Thread32First` / `Thread32Next`. It cannot be decompiled without full VMP unwind; treat it as an opaque `fva_resolve_import(dll, name)` boundary.
- Protobuf method-name strings are stripped/inlined; pin descriptor init via pointer-table scan around 0x7FFBE8215F2C, not via string xrefs.

---

## Русский

Источник: 16 deob_docs сконденсировано в единый 6-дневный план.
Target image: `C:\vmp\FuckVacV2_vmprotect_dump_20260707_004535_pid59960_7FFF420B0000\FuckVacAgain_rebuild.exe`
Подход: p-code-to-p-code перекомпиляция (Colby57 Triton + Z3), не полный lifter.

### Фиксированные якори

| item                                | значение                            |
|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| OEP RVA                             | 0x122C                              |
| Packer entry                        | 0x3ED6D9                            |
| TLS_Callback_0 RVA                  | 0x374254                            |
| Shared VMP dispatcher               | sub_7FFF424EE1C0 (RVA 0x43E1C0)     |
| Tail-return handler                 | sub_7FFF424D44B7 (RVA 0x4244B7)     |
| IAT NtProtectVirtualMemory          | RVA 0x23B020                        |
| Версия VMP                          | 3.6-3.8 (или 3.9.4 по одному доку)  |

Версия VMP подтверждается проверкой того, выполняется ли AntiDebug после VMEntry.

### Prior-work ledger (из `FuckVacV2_dump.dll`)

- aaron-branded protobuf strings
- MyDriver23 kernel driver + `inject_ldrloaddll.ps1`
- `plugin_offsets.h` / `symbols.json`
- `implementation_theory/reconstruction/docs/FVA_CHECKLIST.md`
- `HOOK_A_DECOMP.md`

Этот план продвигает ledger от «портировано из disasm» до «портировано из pseudo-C».

### Блокирующее ограничение

**5.6 kHz `NtReadVirtualMemory` self-scan на `.text`** убивает user-mode inline hooks за секунды. Варианты:
- Kernel-mode shadow через `km_driver.c` lazy-init IOCTL surface, ЛИБО
- Только hardware breakpoints.

### План по дням

#### День 1 — Захват OEP + Scylla dump + скан VMEntry сайтов

1. **Захват OEP через mydbg stealth**
   `C:\vmp\dbg\mydbg(1).exe --stealth --attach-name cs2`
   INT3 / HW BP на `image_base + 0x122C`. Поставить deferred `bpdll FuckVacAgain.dll` ДО инжекта.
   Fallback settled-сигнал: 8 s zero-hits на NtProtectVirtualMemory IAT slot 0x23B020 ПОСЛЕ того как packer entry 0x3ED6D9 хотя бы раз сработал.

2. **Scylla dump распакованного FVA**
   Plugins -> Scylla в x64dbg (или `dump.module` MCP tool):
   - Выбрать процесс `cs2.exe`, модуль `FuckVacAgain.dll`.
   - IAT Autosearch; validate что попал в `.=ly` (RVA 0x23B000, size 0x755).
   - Get Imports; вырезать red thunks.
   - Fix Dump с OEP override 0x1228.
   - Сохранить как `C:\vmp\fva_livecap\FuckVacAgain_scylla_unpacked.dll`.
   - Пока НЕ вырезать секции `.#I5` / `.#?n` / `.=ly`.

3. **Скан VMEntry сайтов** в IDA 8.4+ по `rebuild.exe.i64`:
   Паттерн:
   `53 48 BB ?? ?? ?? ?? ?? ?? ?? ?? C7 44 24 08 ?? ?? ?? ?? 48 8B 1C 24 E8`
   (push rbx; movabs rbx, imm64; mov [rsp+8], imm32; mov rbx, [rsp]; call).
   Fingerprint версии VMP: наличие `NtQueryInformationProcess` / `NtSetInformationThread` в пределах 64 Б после call => VMP >= 3.6 (extra AntiDebug layer); отсутствие => <= 3.5.

#### День 2 — Таксономический скан хендлеров

Скан по `.#I5` и `.#?n` с использованием 10 канонических имён хендлеров:

- vEntry_Prologue
- vDispatcher_JmpR9
- vTapeFetch
- vValueCryptor_Chain
- vOperandDecrypt_XorDword
- vStateStore_MovStack
- vCPUID
- vBranch_Struct
- vExit_Epilogue_Oreans
- vCmp_Oreans_Stock

Также скан `FF 27` (jmp [rdi]) tail и `41 FF E1` (jmp r9) как параллельных вариантов dispatcher'а. Выход: `day2_handlers.json`.

#### День 3 — Live trace

Инжектим FVA через `C:\Users\sshunko\...` PowerShell wrapper (`inject_ldrloaddll.ps1`). Собираем Triton trace через shared dispatcher `sub_7FFF424EE1C0` (17+ callers) с per-caller chunking'ом на tail-return canary в RVA 0x4244B7. Коррелируем с таксономией хендлеров Day 2.

#### День 4 - 6

Не полностью зафиксировано в исходной сводке (input truncated). Стандартное продолжение Colby57:
- Day 4: символическая p-code эмиссия per-handler.
- Day 5: Z3-driven упрощение + JIT-friendly пересборка `.#I5` / `.#?n`.
- Day 6: parity diff против VLB + эмиссия финального pseudo-C в `SafetyPlugin_recovered/`.

### Предупреждения

- 5.6 kHz self-scan требует kernel shadow или HW BPs; не пытаться делать user-mode inline hooks на `.text` FVA.
- VMP dynamic import resolver `sub_7FFBE82ED25E` (1.7 MB) — единственное место, ссылающееся на `SetThreadContext` / `SuspendThread` / `Thread32First` / `Thread32Next`. Не может быть декомпилирован без полного VMP unwind; относиться к нему как к opaque `fva_resolve_import(dll, name)` boundary.
- Protobuf method-name strings stripped/inlined; закреплять descriptor init через pointer-table скан около 0x7FFBE8215F2C, а не через string xrefs.

```

`source/dlls/SafetyPlugin_recovered/docs/protobuf_schema.md`:

```md
# Protobuf schema (usercmd.proto family)

**[EN](#english) · [RU / Русский](#русский)**

---

## English

Recovered from the descriptor blob at `0x7FFBE8210A00..0x7FFBE82163xx`. Message-name and method-name strings (`CSGOUserCmdPB`, `SerializeToArray`, `MergeFrom`, `ParseFrom`, `CopyFrom`, `SerializePartialToArray`) have **zero code xrefs** in the rebuild — they are stripped/inlined. Pinning must be done via the vtable-pointer table around `0x7FFBE8215F2C`.

### Messages

#### CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB
descriptor @ `0x7FFBE8210A3A`; no code xrefs.

| 14  | sv_interp1              | CSGOInterpolationInfoPB       |                  |
| 15  | player_interp           | CSGOInterpolationInfoPB       |                  |

| 65  | target_ent_index        | int32, default = -1           |                  |
| 66  | shoot_position          | CMsgVector                    |                  |
| 67  | target_head_pos_check   | CMsgVector                    |                  |
| 68  | target_abs_pos_check    | CMsgVector                    |                  |
| 69  | target_abs_ang_check    | CMsgQAngle                    |                  |

- qword @ +24
- byte  @ +32
- 5 dwords @ +36..+52

Factory: `CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB::New` @ RVA 0x742730, size 120 B. Signature `B9 78` is not unique — disambiguate via RTTI (memory note: `fva-targets-input-history`). Byte 4 of the ctor sled is `0x10`, not `0x08` (memory note: `cs2-t-new-rva-hardcoded`).

### Gap notes (from the protobuf schema agent)

1. The rebuild has **no code xrefs from message-name strings or vtable RTTI strings** — the file only contains the FileDescriptorProto data blob (`usercmd.proto` @ `0x7FFBE8215F83`, `networkbasetypes.proto` reference). Protobuf runtime symbols (`SerializeToArray` / `MergeFrom` / `ParseFrom` / `CopyFrom` / `SerializePartialToArray`) are **absent as string literals** — hint that CS2 is linked with lite protobuf or that method-name strings were stripped/inlined.
2. Message-name strings `CSGOUserCmdPB`, `CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB`, `CSubtickMoveStep` and RTTI type descriptors `.?AVCSGOUserCmdPB@@` etc. all report 0 xrefs. Cannot pin descriptor init function via string reference. Pattern-match on descriptor blob start address or vtable pointers at `~0x7FFBE8215F2C` instead.

---

## Русский

Восстановлено из descriptor-блоба на `0x7FFBE8210A00..0x7FFBE82163xx`. Строки имён сообщений и методов (`CSGOUserCmdPB`, `SerializeToArray`, `MergeFrom`, `ParseFrom`, `CopyFrom`, `SerializePartialToArray`) имеют **ноль code xrefs** в rebuild'e — они stripped/inlined. Закрепление должно делаться через vtable-pointer таблицу около `0x7FFBE8215F2C`.

### Gap-заметки (от protobuf schema-агента)

1. В rebuild'e **нет code xrefs от строк имён сообщений или RTTI-строк vtable'ов** — файл содержит только FileDescriptorProto data blob (`usercmd.proto` @ `0x7FFBE8215F83`, ссылка на `networkbasetypes.proto`). Runtime-символы protobuf'а (`SerializeToArray` / `MergeFrom` / `ParseFrom` / `CopyFrom` / `SerializePartialToArray`) **отсутствуют как строковые литералы** — намёк на то, что CS2 линкуется с lite protobuf'ом или method-name строки stripped/inlined.
2. Строки имён сообщений `CSGOUserCmdPB`, `CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB`, `CSubtickMoveStep` и RTTI type descriptors `.?AVCSGOUserCmdPB@@` и т.д. — все 0 xrefs. Не можем закрепить descriptor init function через string-ссылку. Pattern-match на descriptor blob start address или vtable pointers на `~0x7FFBE8215F2C` вместо этого.

`source/dlls/SafetyPlugin_recovered/docs/vlb_gap.md`:

```md
# FVA <-> VLB parity gap analysis

**[EN](#english) · [RU / Русский](#русский)**

---

## English

Cross-check between the FVA install sites (from `fulldiff_client_dll.csv`) and the VLB port. Row references are to `fulldiff_client_dll.csv`.

### H1 CreateMove-inner  (client.dll+0xACEF90 live / +0xB09528 current)

- **in_fva:** true
- **in_vlb:** true
- **status:** MATCH (target + logic)
- **install:** FVA installs 5-byte JMP at client.dll+0xACEF90 (row 2: `E9 4B 20 52 FF` vs orig `85 D2 0F 85 85`).
- **VLB parity:** `create_move_hook.cpp` is a monolithic 1:1 port of `sub_7FFBE80C9D8C` with explicit +offset comments (`A.1..K.2`, section D `alt_symbol`, section J viewangle wrap).
- **VLB additions not present in FVA:** SEH gates, `engine2_stable` gate, `FVA_GATE_MODE_ATTACK` / `FVA_GATE_MODE_BYTE` toggle.
- **Observable side effects match:** cvar zero, alt-byte zero, subtick emit, viewangle echo, scratch mirror, move_crc regen.
- **Anchor:** depot 24134959 anchor `48 8B C4 44 88 40 18 89 50 10 48 89 48 08 55 53` matches VLB's `k_target_anchor`.

### H2 IGameSystem::LevelInit  (client.dll+0xAFDFB0 live / +0xB3A600 current)

- **in_fva:** true
- **in_vlb:** true
- **status:** MATCH (target + logic)
- **install:** FVA installs 5-byte JMP at client.dll+0xAFDFB0 (row 3: `E9 DE 2F 4F FF` vs orig `40 55 56 41 56`).
- **VLB parity:** `level_init_hook.cpp` calls `g_original` FIRST (matches FVA order), then `game_state::force_reresolve`, `alt_symbol_reset`, `engine2::notify_level_init_complete`.
- **Comment source:** workflow wq0rgtslf audit.

### H3 CBaseUserCmdPB::SerializePartialToArray  (client.dll+0x1189930 live / +0x11AD4E0 current)

- **in_fva:** true
- **in_vlb:** true
- **status:** MATCH (target + logic)
- **install:** FVA installs 5-byte JMP at client.dll+0x1189930 (row 4: `E9 1E 76 E6 FE` vs orig `48 89 5C 24 18`).
- **VLB parity:** `serialize_to_array_hook.cpp` — capture-only, vtable-RVA fingerprint distinguishes CBaseUserCmdPB from siblings (LazyString wrapper broke `GetTypeName`).
- **Corpus proof of target message family:** `CInButtonStatePB` dtor (`sub_7FFBE80F80F0`), `CSGOUserCmdPB::MergeFrom` (`sub_7FFBE80F853C` line 507), `CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB::MergeFrom` (`sub_7FFBE80F8B4C` line 879).

### H4 ShouldUpdateSequences (animationsystem.dll)

- **in_fva:** PLAUSIBLE (VMP-encapsulated inside sub_7FFBE80C9D8C's tail)
- **in_vlb:** true (`animation_hook.cpp`)
- **status:** **CONFIRMED via runtime memory read (2026-07-16)**
- **Runtime verification** (cs2 PID 53000, animationsystem.dll base 0x7ff906070000):
  - VLB sig `48 89 5C 24 08 48 89 74 24 18 57 48 83 EC 20 49 8B 40 48` hits UNIQUELY at `base + 0x14F950` on the current CS2 build.
  - Legacy RVA 0x14EFF0 no longer contains the sig (drift +0x960 = 2400 bytes since the source comment was written).
  - Pattern-scan across full 0xbd9000-byte module returned exactly 1 hit — no false positives.
  - Target function has legit MSVC callee-save prologue with 4-arg fastcall + null-check on `[r8+0x48]`.
- **Historical FVA side:** could not be confirmed statically because livedump has NO `animationsystem.dll` CSV; the actual FVA install site is encapsulated inside the VMP dynamic-resolver blob (`sub_7FFBE82ED25E`). But the TARGET FUNCTION exists and is exactly where VLB expects.
- **Verified by:** `C:\vmp\build\devirt_v2\rt_verify_anim_hook.py` — reproducible on any future depot.

### H_DynamicResolver_Internal (self)

- **in_fva:** PLAUSIBLE
- **in_vlb:** n/a
- **status:** VMP-BLOCKED
- **Reason:** `sub_7FFBE82ED25E` (1.7 MB) is the only site referencing `SetThreadContext` / `SuspendThread` / `Thread32First` / `Thread32Next`. IAT slots `NtProtectVirtualMemory` / `VirtualProtect` / `NtAllocateVirtualMemory` have **zero direct xrefs** and must be resolved through here.
- **Consequence:** treat it as an opaque `fva_resolve_import(dll, name)` boundary in all reconstruction code. Not a target-process hook.

### Cross-cutting

- Section-J (input_history angle wrap) targets `CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB.view_angles` (proto tag 2) via vtable RVA `0x1A1E028`. It does **NOT** touch `subtick_moves` (proto tag 18 on CBaseUserCmdPB). Any VLB path that mutates subtick_moves is a divergence — flag it here.
- The FVA-only control is `byte_7FFBE823A9A0`. VLB exposes an equivalent semantic via `FVA_GATE_MODE_BYTE`; the `FVA_GATE_MODE_ATTACK` variant in VLB has no FVA counterpart (VLB-added policy layer).
- Per memory note `fva-no-hotkey`: reconstruction code MUST NOT reintroduce a `g_target_armed` variable; self-echo is a **delta=0 write into the existing entry**, NOT an append.

---

## Русский

Cross-check между FVA install-сайтами (из `fulldiff_client_dll.csv`) и VLB-портом. Row-ссылки — на `fulldiff_client_dll.csv`.

### H1 CreateMove-inner  (client.dll+0xACEF90 live / +0xB09528 текущий)

- **in_fva:** true
- **in_vlb:** true
- **status:** MATCH (target + логика)
- **install:** FVA ставит 5-byte JMP на client.dll+0xACEF90 (строка 2: `E9 4B 20 52 FF` vs orig `85 D2 0F 85 85`).
- **VLB parity:** `create_move_hook.cpp` — монолитный 1:1 порт `sub_7FFBE80C9D8C` с явными +offset комментариями (`A.1..K.2`, section D `alt_symbol`, section J viewangle wrap).
- **VLB additions отсутствующие в FVA:** SEH gates, `engine2_stable` gate, `FVA_GATE_MODE_ATTACK` / `FVA_GATE_MODE_BYTE` toggle.
- **Observable side effects совпадают:** cvar zero, alt-byte zero, subtick emit, viewangle echo, scratch mirror, move_crc regen.
- **Anchor:** depot 24134959 anchor `48 8B C4 44 88 40 18 89 50 10 48 89 48 08 55 53` совпадает с VLB `k_target_anchor`.

### H2 IGameSystem::LevelInit  (client.dll+0xAFDFB0 live / +0xB3A600 текущий)

- **in_fva:** true
- **in_vlb:** true
- **status:** MATCH (target + логика)
- **install:** FVA ставит 5-byte JMP на client.dll+0xAFDFB0 (строка 3: `E9 DE 2F 4F FF` vs orig `40 55 56 41 56`).
- **VLB parity:** `level_init_hook.cpp` вызывает `g_original` СНАЧАЛА (совпадает с порядком FVA), затем `game_state::force_reresolve`, `alt_symbol_reset`, `engine2::notify_level_init_complete`.
- **Comment source:** workflow wq0rgtslf audit.

### H3 CBaseUserCmdPB::SerializePartialToArray  (client.dll+0x1189930 live / +0x11AD4E0 текущий)

- **in_fva:** true
- **in_vlb:** true
- **status:** MATCH (target + логика)
- **install:** FVA ставит 5-byte JMP на client.dll+0x1189930 (строка 4: `E9 1E 76 E6 FE` vs orig `48 89 5C 24 18`).
- **VLB parity:** `serialize_to_array_hook.cpp` — capture-only, vtable-RVA fingerprint отличает CBaseUserCmdPB от siblings (LazyString wrapper сломал `GetTypeName`).
- **Corpus доказательство target message family:** `CInButtonStatePB` dtor (`sub_7FFBE80F80F0`), `CSGOUserCmdPB::MergeFrom` (`sub_7FFBE80F853C` строка 507), `CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB::MergeFrom` (`sub_7FFBE80F8B4C` строка 879).

### H4 ShouldUpdateSequences (animationsystem.dll)

- **in_fva:** PLAUSIBLE (VMP-инкапсулирован внутри tail'а sub_7FFBE80C9D8C)
- **in_vlb:** true (`animation_hook.cpp`)
- **status:** **CONFIRMED через runtime memory read (2026-07-16)**
- **Runtime верификация** (cs2 PID 53000, animationsystem.dll base 0x7ff906070000):
  - VLB sig `48 89 5C 24 08 48 89 74 24 18 57 48 83 EC 20 49 8B 40 48` попадает УНИКАЛЬНО на `base + 0x14F950` на текущем CS2 build'e.
  - Legacy RVA 0x14EFF0 больше не содержит sig (drift +0x960 = 2400 байт с момента написания source comment'а).
  - Pattern-scan через полный 0xbd9000-байтный модуль вернул ровно 1 попадание — никаких false positives.
  - Target функция имеет легитимный MSVC callee-save prologue с 4-arg fastcall + null-check на `[r8+0x48]`.
- **Историческая FVA сторона:** не могла быть подтверждена статически потому что livedump НЕ имеет CSV `animationsystem.dll`; фактический FVA install-сайт инкапсулирован внутри VMP dynamic-resolver блоба (`sub_7FFBE82ED25E`). Но TARGET FUNCTION существует и находится точно там, где VLB ожидает.
- **Verified by:** `C:\vmp\build\devirt_v2\rt_verify_anim_hook.py` — воспроизводимо на любом будущем depot'e.

### H_DynamicResolver_Internal (self)

- **in_fva:** PLAUSIBLE
- **in_vlb:** n/a
- **status:** VMP-BLOCKED
- **Причина:** `sub_7FFBE82ED25E` (1.7 MB) — единственный сайт, ссылающийся на `SetThreadContext` / `SuspendThread` / `Thread32First` / `Thread32Next`. IAT slot'ы `NtProtectVirtualMemory` / `VirtualProtect` / `NtAllocateVirtualMemory` имеют **ноль прямых xrefs** и должны resolve'иться через здесь.
- **Следствие:** относиться к нему как к opaque `fva_resolve_import(dll, name)` boundary во всём reconstruction-коде. Не target-process hook.

### Cross-cutting

- Section-J (input_history angle wrap) целит `CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB.view_angles` (proto tag 2) через vtable RVA `0x1A1E028`. Он **НЕ** трогает `subtick_moves` (proto tag 18 на CBaseUserCmdPB). Любой VLB-путь мутирующий subtick_moves — divergence, помечать здесь.
- FVA-only контроль — `byte_7FFBE823A9A0`. VLB экспозит эквивалентную семантику через `FVA_GATE_MODE_BYTE`; вариант `FVA_GATE_MODE_ATTACK` в VLB не имеет FVA counterpart'а (VLB-added policy layer).
- Согласно memory-заметке `fva-no-hotkey`: reconstruction-код НЕ ДОЛЖЕН заново вводить переменную `g_target_armed`; self-echo — это **delta=0 write в существующий entry**, НЕ append.

```

`source/dlls/SafetyPlugin_recovered/hooks/create_move_hook.pseudo.cpp`:

```cpp
// ============================================================================
// create_move_hook.pseudo.cpp   H1_CreateMove
//
// Recovered from rebuild.i64 decompilation; not verified to compile.
// Target: client.dll+0xACEF90 (live) / +0xB09528 (current depot)
// FVA symbol: sub_7FFBE80C9D8C  (Hook A body, "CreateMove-inner")
// Install:   inline 5-byte JMP rel32   E9 4B 20 52 FF   (orig 85 D2 0F 85 85)
// Gate byte: byte_7FFBE823A9A0  (init @ 0x7FFBE80CA00B, check @ 0x7FFBE80CA6B4)
// Cross-ref: VLB create_move_hook.cpp   (1:1 port + SEH + engine2_stable +
//                                        FVA_GATE_MODE_ATTACK/BYTE wrappers
//                                        that are NOT present in FVA)
// ============================================================================

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// ORIGINAL DECOMPILED BODY (excerpt, sub_7FFBE80C9D8C)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
/*
sub_7FFBE80C9D8C proc near
    ; ---- prologue ---------------------------------------------------------
    push    rbp
    push    rsi
    push    rdi
    push    r12..r15
    sub     rsp, ...
    xor     r13d, r13d           ; r13b = 0 (used later as gate cmp operand)

    ; ---- 0x7FFBE80CA00B  gate-byte lazy init (TLS-once + FNV1a) ------------
    ; call    sub_7FFBE80FB1C4    ; decrypt dword_7FFBE823A9CC (key 0xB6E8F068409FEF6C)
    ; mov     cl, byte ptr [rax+rdi]           ; rax = decoded RVA table base
    ; mov     cs:byte_7FFBE823A9A0, cl         ; <-- gate byte written from manifest
    ; mov     [rax+rbx], dil                    ; dil == 0 -> zero a companion slot

    ; ... body ~ 900 bytes of scratch mirror / cvar zero / alt_symbol / ...

    ; ---- 0x7FFBE80CA6B4  Section-J gate check ------------------------------
    ; cmp     cs:byte_7FFBE823A9A0, r13b
    ; jz      loc_7FFBE80CAA2F                  ; if byte == 0 -> skip Section-J

    ; ---- 0x7FFBE80CA6C1  Section-J: view-angle wrap fixup ------------------
    ; mov     rbx, cs:qword_7FFBE823C250        ; reference angle source
    ; mov     rsi, cs:qword_7FFBE823A918        ; CSGOUserCmdPB / input state ctx
    ; or      dword ptr [rsi+20h], r15d         ; set PB flag word bit
    ; mov     rdi, [rsi+40h]                    ; input_history entry ptr
    ; test    rdi, rdi
    ; jnz     have_entry
    ; call    sub_7FFBE80FAD0C                  ; lazy-allocate input_history entry
    ; have_entry:
    ; mov     rdi, [rdi+40h]                    ; sub-object
    ; call    sub_7FFBE80F6FEC                  ; deref helper
    ; movss   xmm0, dword ptr [rdi+18h]         ; view_angles.pitch
    ; movss   xmm1, dword ptr [rdi+1Ch]         ; view_angles.yaw
    ; movss   xmm2, dword ptr [rdi+20h]         ; view_angles.roll
    ; subss   xmm0, dword ptr [rbx]
    ; subss   xmm1, dword ptr [rbx+4]
    ; subss   xmm2, dword ptr [rbx+8]
    ; ; per-axis wrap into [-180,180) via sub/vcomiss/vaddss against
    ; ;    dword_7FFBE82190E8 = 180.0f
    ; ;    dword_7FFBE82190EC = 360.0f
    ; ;    dword_7FFBE82190F4 = -180.0f
    ; ; using sub_7FFBE81B3D60 as fmod-like helper.
    ; ...
sub_7FFBE80C9D8C endp
*/

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// TYPE-ANNOTATED PSEUDO-C SKELETON
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef>
#include <cstring>

// ---- forward-decl of engine primitives referenced by name -----------------
struct CUserCmd;               // opaque; layout follows CSGOUserCmdPB below
struct CSGOUserCmdPB;          // protobuf state ctx  (@ qword_7FFBE823A918)
struct CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB;// factory @ RVA 0x742730, size 120
struct QAngle { float pitch, yaw, roll; };

// ---- FVA globals (relative to image base 0x7FFBE8000000) ------------------
extern uint8_t   g_gate_byte;                 // byte_7FFBE823A9A0
extern QAngle*   g_reference_angles;          // qword_7FFBE823C250
extern CSGOUserCmdPB* g_user_cmd_ctx;         // qword_7FFBE823A918
extern uint32_t  g_rva_cache_dword;           // dword_7FFBE823A9CC (FNV1a-encoded)
static constexpr uint64_t kRvaCacheKey = 0xB6E8F068409FEF6Cull;

// ---- FVA helpers ----------------------------------------------------------
extern "C" uint64_t                       fva_fnv1a_decrypt(uint32_t enc, uint64_t key);        // sub_7FFBE80FB1C4
extern "C" CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB*       fva_lazy_alloc_input_history_entry(CSGOUserCmdPB*);   // sub_7FFBE80FAD0C
extern "C" void*                          fva_input_entry_subobject(CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB*);  // sub_7FFBE80F6FEC
extern "C" float                          fva_fmod_like_wrap(float x, float mod);               // sub_7FFBE81B3D60

// ---- gate-byte lazy init (0x7FFBE80CA00B) --------------------------------
static void fva_gate_byte_init_once(size_t rdi_index, size_t rbx_zero_index) {
    // TLS-once elided; VMP wraps this in a per-thread guard.
    uint8_t* table = reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(
        fva_fnv1a_decrypt(g_rva_cache_dword, kRvaCacheKey));
    g_gate_byte      = table[rdi_index];        // <-- populate gate byte from manifest
    table[rbx_zero_index] = 0;                  // zero companion slot
}

// ---- Section-J: input_history view-angle wrap fixup ----------------------
static void fva_section_j_apply_angle_wrap() {
    // Precondition (checked by caller): g_gate_byte != 0
    CSGOUserCmdPB* ctx = g_user_cmd_ctx;
    // OR a PB flag bit into ctx+0x20 (r15d source)
    *reinterpret_cast<uint32_t*>(reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(ctx) + 0x20) |= /*r15d*/ 0u;

    CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB* entry =
        *reinterpret_cast<CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB**>(
            reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(ctx) + 0x40);
    if (!entry) {
        entry = fva_lazy_alloc_input_history_entry(ctx);
    }
    QAngle* view = reinterpret_cast<QAngle*>(
        static_cast<uint8_t*>(fva_input_entry_subobject(entry)) + 0x18);
    QAngle delta;
    delta.pitch = view->pitch - g_reference_angles->pitch;
    delta.yaw   = view->yaw   - g_reference_angles->yaw;
    delta.roll  = view->roll  - g_reference_angles->roll;

    // Per-axis wrap into [-180, 180)
    auto wrap = [](float x) -> float {
        constexpr float k180  =  180.0f;   // dword_7FFBE82190E8
        constexpr float k360  =  360.0f;   // dword_7FFBE82190EC
        constexpr float kN180 = -180.0f;   // dword_7FFBE82190F4
        (void)k180; (void)kN180;
        return fva_fmod_like_wrap(x + k180, k360) - k180;
    };
    view->pitch = wrap(delta.pitch);
    view->yaw   = wrap(delta.yaw);
    view->roll  = wrap(delta.roll);
}

// ---- Hook body: sub_7FFBE80C9D8C (CreateMove-inner) ----------------------
extern "C" void FVA_HookA_CreateMoveInner(void* rcx_this, void* rdx_state /*, ... */) {
    // (A) prologue scratch mirror, cvar zero, alt_symbol handling
    //     -> ~900 B of ungated logic that matches VLB sections A/B/C/D/E/F/G/H/I
    //     (see VLB create_move_hook.cpp for the per-section breakdown).

    // (B) gate-byte lazy init  (0x7FFBE80CA00B)
    static bool s_gate_inited = false;
    if (!s_gate_inited) {
        fva_gate_byte_init_once(/*rdi_index=*/0, /*rbx_zero_index=*/0);
        s_gate_inited = true;
    }

    // (C) Section-J gate check  (0x7FFBE80CA6B4)
    if (g_gate_byte == 0) {
        // JZ loc_7FFBE80CAA2F  -> skip ~900 B of reconstruction
        return;
    }

    // (D) Section-J body  (0x7FFBE80CA6C1)
    fva_section_j_apply_angle_wrap();
}

// ===========================================================================
// VLB PARITY NOTES  (see docs/vlb_gap.md)
//
// VLB create_move_hook.cpp is a monolithic 1:1 port of sub_7FFBE80C9D8C with
// explicit "+offset" comments (A.1..K.2, section D alt_symbol, section J
// viewangle wrap). VLB additionally wraps the body in:
//     - SEH __try/__except
//     - engine2_stable gate
//     - FVA_GATE_MODE_ATTACK / FVA_GATE_MODE_BYTE toggle
// None of these are present in FVA itself; the observable side effects
// (cvar zero, alt-byte zero, subtick emit, viewangle echo, scratch mirror,
// move_crc regen) all match.
//
// Depot 24134959 anchor bytes
//     48 8B C4 44 88 40 18 89 50 10 48 89 48 08 55 53
// match VLB's k_target_anchor.
// ===========================================================================

```

`source/dlls/SafetyPlugin_recovered/hooks/level_init_hook.pseudo.cpp`:

```cpp
// ============================================================================
// level_init_hook.pseudo.cpp   H2_LevelInit
//
// Recovered from rebuild.i64 decompilation; not verified to compile.
// Target: client.dll+0xAFDFB0 (live) / +0xB3A600 (current depot)
// FVA symbol: IGameSystem::LevelInit dispatch  (FVA-labeled 'CreateSwapChain'
//                                               decoy in the corpus)
// Install:   inline 5-byte JMP rel32   E9 DE 2F 4F FF   (orig 40 55 56 41 56)
// Cross-ref: VLB level_init_hook.cpp   (calls g_original FIRST, then
//                                       force_reresolve + alt_symbol_reset;
//                                       matches FVA order per workflow
//                                       wq0rgtslf audit)
// ============================================================================

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// ORIGINAL DECOMPILED BODY (skeleton; the FVA host body is short — it wraps
// the game's LevelInit and then triggers per-level re-resolve work.)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
/*
FVA_HookB_LevelInit proc near
    push    rbp
    push    rsi
    push    r14
    sub     rsp, ...
    ; (1) call original IGameSystem::LevelInit  ------------------------------
    call    g_original_LevelInit
    ; (2) trigger post-load re-resolve  --------------------------------------
    call    game_state__force_reresolve
    ; (3) reset alt_symbol table if byte_A97C == 0  --------------------------
    cmp     cs:byte_7FFBE823A97C, 0
    jnz     skip_alt_reset
    call    alt_symbol_reset
skip_alt_reset:
    ; (4) engine2 notify  -----------------------------------------------------
    call    engine2__notify_level_init_complete
    add     rsp, ...
    pop     r14
    pop     rsi
    pop     rbp
    retn
FVA_HookB_LevelInit endp
*/

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// TYPE-ANNOTATED PSEUDO-C SKELETON
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef>

struct IGameSystem;
struct EventLevelInit_t;      // opaque; forwarded verbatim to the original

extern "C" void (*g_original_LevelInit)(IGameSystem*      self,
                                        EventLevelInit_t* evt);

// FVA globals
extern uint8_t g_alt_symbol_flag;   // byte_7FFBE823A97C  (0 => reset table)

// FVA post-load helpers (names paraphrased from corpus / VLB parity notes)
extern "C" void fva_game_state_force_reresolve();
extern "C" void fva_alt_symbol_reset();
extern "C" void fva_engine2_notify_level_init_complete();

// ---- hook body ------------------------------------------------------------
extern "C" void FVA_HookB_LevelInit(IGameSystem*      self,
                                    EventLevelInit_t* evt) {
    // (1) call the original FIRST — this ordering is the exact parity the
    //     VLB port was audited against (workflow wq0rgtslf).
    g_original_LevelInit(self, evt);

    // (2) per-level symbol re-resolve
    fva_game_state_force_reresolve();

    // (3) alt-symbol reset gated on byte_7FFBE823A97C == 0
    if (g_alt_symbol_flag == 0) {
        fva_alt_symbol_reset();
    }

    // (4) engine2 notify
    fva_engine2_notify_level_init_complete();
}

// ===========================================================================
// VLB PARITY NOTES  (see docs/vlb_gap.md)
//
// VLB level_init_hook.cpp:
//   - calls g_original FIRST  (matches FVA order)
//   - then game_state::force_reresolve
//   - then alt_symbol_reset gated on byte_A97C == 0
//   - then engine2::notify_level_init_complete
// Workflow wq0rgtslf audit confirmed parity fixes:
//   - "resolve-once" semantics (do not re-resolve mid-frame)
//   - alt_symbol_reset only when the FVA gate flag is clear
// ===========================================================================

```

`source/dlls/SafetyPlugin_recovered/hooks/serialize_to_array_hook.pseudo.cpp`:

```cpp
// ============================================================================
// serialize_to_array_hook.pseudo.cpp   H3_SerializePartialToArray
//
// Recovered from rebuild.i64 decompilation; not verified to compile.
// Target: client.dll+0x1189930 (live) / +0x11AD4E0 (current depot)
// FVA symbol: CBaseUserCmdPB::SerializePartialToArray (usercmd.pb.cpp:1891)
// Install:   inline 5-byte JMP rel32   E9 1E 76 E6 FE   (orig 48 89 5C 24 18)
// Cross-ref: VLB serialize_to_array_hook.cpp  (capture-only, vtable-RVA
//                                              fingerprint distinguishes
//                                              CBaseUserCmdPB from siblings)
// ============================================================================

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// ORIGINAL DECOMPILED BODY (context; the hook is capture-only, so the FVA
// side does not reimplement SerializePartialToArray. Relevant corpus symbols
// for the CBaseUserCmdPB family are listed here for pinning.)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
/*
CInButtonStatePB dtor / vftable  @ sub_7FFBE80F80F0   (usercmd.pb.cpp:350)
CSGOUserCmdPB::MergeFrom          @ sub_7FFBE80F853C   (usercmd.pb.cpp:507)
  copies 3 qword ptr fields at +0x18 / +0x20 / +0x28
  (input_history list head, base cmd, mv_cmd)
CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB::MergeFrom@ sub_7FFBE80F8B4C   (usercmd.pb.cpp:879)
  MergeFrom mask 0x7F copying 7 fields:
    qword @ +24, byte @ +32, 5 dwords @ +36..+52
protobuf_merge_submessage         @ sub_7FFBE80E41B0
  universal sub-message merge tail-call reached by every MergeFrom variant

FileDescriptorProto for usercmd.proto @ 0x7FFBE8210A00..0x7FFBE82163xx
  (descriptor blob present; NO xrefs from method-name strings; must pin via
  vtable pointer table around 0x7FFBE8215F2C)
*/

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// TYPE-ANNOTATED PSEUDO-C SKELETON  (capture-only hook stub)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef>

// ---- opaque protobuf shapes (see docs/protobuf_schema.md) -----------------
struct CBaseUserCmdPB;
struct CSGOUserCmdPB;
struct CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB;
struct CInButtonStatePB;

// Trampoline slot for original (installed by the FVA installer).
extern "C" uint8_t* (*g_orig_SerializePartialToArray)(CBaseUserCmdPB* self,
                                                     uint8_t*        out_buf);

// vtable-RVA fingerprint (see H3 evidence): LazyString wrapper broke
// GetTypeName so we cannot use the message-type-name string. Instead the
// installer records the vtable RVA of the CBaseUserCmdPB it saw at install
// time and compares against it on every call.
extern "C" uintptr_t g_expected_vtable_rva;

static inline uintptr_t vtable_rva_of(const void* obj, uintptr_t image_base) {
    uintptr_t vt = *reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t const*>(obj);
    return vt - image_base;
}

// ---- hook body (capture-only) ---------------------------------------------
extern "C" uint8_t* FVA_HookC_SerializePartialToArray(CBaseUserCmdPB* self,
                                                     uint8_t*        out_buf)
{
    // (1) fingerprint filter: only capture true CBaseUserCmdPB messages.
    //     Sibling classes in the usercmd.pb family (CSGOUserCmdPB,
    //     CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB, CInButtonStatePB, CSubtickMoveStep) reach
    //     the same trampoline via inheritance; the vtable-RVA fingerprint
    //     is the only reliable discriminator we have.
    extern uintptr_t fva_image_base_client_dll();
    const uintptr_t vt_rva = vtable_rva_of(self, fva_image_base_client_dll());
    if (vt_rva != g_expected_vtable_rva) {
        return g_orig_SerializePartialToArray(self, out_buf);
    }

    // (2) call the original — no mutation.
    uint8_t* end = g_orig_SerializePartialToArray(self, out_buf);

    // (3) capture the serialized frame for the reconstruction pipeline.
    //     Buffer: [out_buf, end).
    //     FVA emits this to its ring buffer via a helper that ultimately
    //     resolves through the VMP dynamic import resolver
    //     sub_7FFBE82ED25E; the resolved call target is not decompilable.
    extern void fva_capture_usercmd_frame(const uint8_t* begin, const uint8_t* end,
                                          const CBaseUserCmdPB* src);
    fva_capture_usercmd_frame(out_buf, end, self);

    return end;
}

// ===========================================================================
// VLB PARITY NOTES  (see docs/vlb_gap.md)
//
// VLB serialize_to_array_hook.cpp is functionally identical: capture-only,
// vtable-RVA fingerprint, no mutation of the serialized bytes. VLB uses the
// same "call original first, then capture" order.
//
// Ring-buffer sink and downstream reconstruction pipeline (input_history
// spoof driven by byte_7FFBE823A9A0) live in
//   core/input_history_spoof.pseudo.cpp
// and are gated inside the CreateMove hook, not this one.
// ===========================================================================

```

`source/dlls/VacLiveBypass/CMakeLists.txt`:

```txt
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)
project(FvaReconstruction LANGUAGES CXX C)

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_MSVC_RUNTIME_LIBRARY "MultiThreadedDLL")

# --- Vendored MinHook (self-contained, no skeleton coupling) -----------------
add_subdirectory(ext/minhook)

add_library(fva_recon SHARED
    src/dllmain.cpp

    src/core/signature_scanner.cpp
    src/core/convar.cpp
    src/core/logf.cpp
    src/core/remote_offsets.cpp

    src/valve/client_input.cpp
    src/valve/protobuf_alloc.cpp

    src/features/cvar_suppress.cpp
    src/features/console_exec.cpp

    src/hooks/serialize_to_array_hook.cpp
    src/hooks/create_move_hook.cpp
    src/hooks/local_pawn_gate.cpp
    src/hooks/phase_b2.cpp
    src/hooks/phase_c.cpp
    src/hooks/phase_d.cpp
    src/hooks/animation_hook.cpp
    src/hooks/level_init_hook.cpp
    src/hooks/engine2_bindings.cpp
    src/hooks/game_state_resolver.cpp
    src/hooks/hook_manager.cpp
    src/hooks/capture_buffer.cpp
    src/hooks/scratch_serialize.cpp

    src/tests/self_check.cpp
    src/tests/detect_build.cpp
)

target_include_directories(fva_recon PRIVATE src)

target_compile_options(fva_recon PRIVATE /W4 /arch:AVX2 /permissive-)
target_compile_definitions(fva_recon PRIVATE
    WIN32_LEAN_AND_MEAN
    NOMINMAX
    _CRT_SECURE_NO_WARNINGS
)

# Build with `-DFVA_CONSOLE=ON` to open an AllocConsole on inject for debug.
option(FVA_CONSOLE "Enable AllocConsole on inject" ON)
if(FVA_CONSOLE)
    target_compile_definitions(fva_recon PRIVATE FVA_CONSOLE)
endif()

# Verbose per-tick H1 logging (BUTTONS, subtick_moves.size, has_bits,
# buttons_pb, raw->flags transitions).  Loud (~128 lines/sec) — for reverse
# dev only, causes FPS drop in retail.  Enable: -DFVA_VERBOSE_H1=ON.
option(FVA_VERBOSE_H1 "Per-tick verbose H1 log spam" OFF)
if(FVA_VERBOSE_H1)
    target_compile_definitions(fva_recon PRIVATE FVA_VERBOSE_H1)
endif()

# Target depot: 14167 (baseline) or 24134959 (current post-major-update).
# Selects RVA / MD5 / hook prologue sets in version_manifest.h.
# Default: 24134959.  Rebuild with `cmake -DFVA_TARGET_DEPOT=14167 …` for baseline.
if(NOT DEFINED FVA_TARGET_DEPOT)
    set(FVA_TARGET_DEPOT 24134959 CACHE STRING "Target CS2 depot buildid (14167 or 24134959)")
endif()
if(NOT FVA_TARGET_DEPOT MATCHES "^(14167|24134959)$")
    message(FATAL_ERROR "FVA_TARGET_DEPOT must be 14167 or 24134959, got: ${FVA_TARGET_DEPOT}")
endif()
message(STATUS "Building for CS2 depot ${FVA_TARGET_DEPOT}")
target_compile_definitions(fva_recon PRIVATE FVA_TARGET_DEPOT=${FVA_TARGET_DEPOT})

# Build with `-DFVA_TRACE_HOOKA=ON` to enable PRE/POST state tracing in the
# Hook A detour (every tick logs entry state, phase transitions, exit state).
# Expensive — ~128 log lines per second in gameplay.  Only enable for
# reversal / parity-diff work against FVA.
option(FVA_TRACE_HOOKA "PRE/POST tracing in Hook A detour" OFF)
if(FVA_TRACE_HOOKA)
    target_compile_definitions(fva_recon PRIVATE FVA_TRACE_HOOKA)
endif()

# Section-J gate mode:
#   attack (default) — schema-way through CInButtonStatePB buttonstate1|2|3
#                      & (IN_ATTACK|IN_ATTACK2).  Spoof только при click.
#   byte             — FVA-original 1:1 — spoof когда byte @CCSGOInput+0xA4
#                      latched != 0 при inject (needs external toggle write).
if(NOT DEFINED FVA_GATE_MODE)
    set(FVA_GATE_MODE "attack" CACHE STRING "Section-J gate: attack or byte")
endif()
if(NOT FVA_GATE_MODE MATCHES "^(attack|byte)$")
    message(FATAL_ERROR "FVA_GATE_MODE must be 'attack' or 'byte', got: ${FVA_GATE_MODE}")
endif()
message(STATUS "Section-J gate mode: ${FVA_GATE_MODE}")
if(FVA_GATE_MODE STREQUAL "byte")
    target_compile_definitions(fva_recon PRIVATE FVA_GATE_MODE_BYTE)
endif()

target_link_libraries(fva_recon PRIVATE minhook kernel32 user32 advapi32 bcrypt winhttp)

```

`source/dlls/VacLiveBypass/README.md`:

```md
# VacLiveBypass — CS2 Anti-Aim Reconstruction

**Полностью открытая C++ реконструкция обфусцированного плагина Gamesense/FVA для Counter-Strike 2.** Проект — «чистая» реализация функциональности VMProtect-обфусцированной DLL, полученная методом реверс-инжиниринга: наблюдение живого поведения, декомпиляция через IDA Hex-Rays, побайтовая верификация RVA. Никакого исходного кода оригинала не использовалось — только байтовые сигнатуры и pseudocode из декомпилятора.

Цель — **научная и defensive-security ценность**: показать, как работает viewangles-spoofing / subtick-injection anti-aim без обфускации, чтобы разработчики античитов могли строить детект-логику на паттернах, а не гоняться за конкретной DLL.

---

## Что делает проект

Каждый тик (64 раза в секунду) CS2 отправляет серверу protobuf-сообщение `CBaseUserCmdPB`, описывающее ввод пользователя за прошедший интервал: viewangles, движение, кнопки, subtick-события (моменты когда именно нажали клавиши в течение тика). Наш плагин перехватывает три функции клиента:

1. **`CBaseUserCmd::CreateMove` inner** — заполнение самой команды на клиенте
2. **`IGameSystem::LevelInit`** — старт новой карты (нужен для инвалидирования кэшей)
3. **`CBaseUserCmdPB::SerializePartialToArray`** — сериализация на wire (нужна для диагностики)

Внутри детура **Hook A** мы модифицируем `pb->viewangles` и добавляем сфальсифицированные `CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB` в `raw+0x28` (input_history), эмулируя быстрое вращение мыши между тиками. Сервер CS2 использует эти данные для валидации попаданий: если ты выстрелил "куда попал", сервер откатывает мировое состояние на момент субтика и проверяет trace. Injecting fake input_history entries заставляет сервер строить некорректную модель того, куда игрок смотрел — anti-aim эффект.

Модификации попадают в wire автоматически: CS2's protobuf-сериализация читает наши изменения через тот же путь, что и обычные данные.

---

## Архитектура

### Компоненты

```

├── version_manifest.h             Все RVA / offset'ы / md5 / build fingerprints

```

### Магия — где spoofing происходит

Вся спуф-логика — **~30 строк в `phase_b2.cpp::run_fva_section_j_emitter`**:

```cpp
apply(raw_CUserCmd)                        // src/hooks/phase_b2.cpp
  ├── pb = raw->base                        // CBaseUserCmdPB pointer
  ├── pb->has_bits |= VIEWANGLES            // форсируем сериализацию viewangles
  ├── prior = pb->viewangles                // снимок ДО спуфа
  ├── delta = wrap180(prior - target)       // разница по каждой оси
  └── run_fva_section_j_emitter(...):
        ├── for iter in 0..remaining:
        │     ├── step   = CS2_CSGOInputHistoryEntryPB_New(subs.arena)  // 120B
        │     ├── qangle = CS2_CMsgQAngle_New(nullptr)                  // 40B, heap
        │     ├── qangle.{x,y,z} = prior + fraction*delta                // фейковая ракета
        │     ├── step[+0x18] = qangle                                    // step.viewAngles
        │     ├── step[+0x60..0x68] = tick/fraction                       // FVA константы
        │     ├── step->has_bits |= 0x1E01                                // presence flags
        │     └── AddAllocated fast-path OR add_allocated_slow_path      // insert в rep
        └── обновить reference cache для следующего тика
```

**Wire-observable effect:** сервер видит цепочку subtick'ов "я плавно повернулся с A до B за этот тик" — вместо реального мгновенного щелчка. Античит-логика rewind'а стреляет по неправильному месту → противник не получает урон = anti-aim.

---

## Ключевые технические открытия

### 1. Protobuf `RepeatedPtrField` — swap-append vs grow

FVA `sub_7FFBE80CB348` (наш `add_allocated_slow_path`) имеет **3 ветки**:

- **A: grow** — `!rep || current==total` → выделить новую `Rep` через `IMemAlloc::Alloc`, скопировать элементы, установить `subs->rep=new`
- **B: swap-append** — `allocated<total` → `elements[allocated] = elements[current]; elements[current++] = step; allocated++`
- **C: recycle** — `allocated==total && current<total && arena==0` → перезаписать `elements[current]` без выделения (старый элемент "утекает" — CS2's arena reclaim'ит на следующем reset'е)

Ошибка в раннем порте — заставлять Case C делать grow → вызывало delayed crash cs2 через ~200 tick'ов (CS2 замещал `subs->rep`, но не сбрасывал `subs->total_size` → out-of-bounds reads в networksystem).

### 2. `ArenaStringPtr::Set` ABI

`move_crc` — поле `ArenaStringPtr` (не `std::string*`!), содержит **tagged pointer** с 3 битами:
- bit 0 — `kAllocated`
- bit 1 — `kMutableArena`
- bit 2 — `kDefault`

**Критический bug (task #84):**
```cpp
// НЕПРАВИЛЬНО — передаёт tagged VALUE как this
arena_set(raw->base->move_crc, &s_bytes, arena);

// ПРАВИЛЬНО — передаёт ADDRESS OF SLOT
void* asp_this = static_cast<void*>(&raw->base->move_crc);
arena_set(asp_this, &s_bytes, arena);
```

Первый вариант crash'ил cs2 через ~10 сек (partial memory write повреждал tagged_ptr layout). Второй — корректный `this` = адрес поля.

### 3. Arena word untag

FVA использует специальную схему тегирования указателей арены (в `msg + 8` word):
- bit 1 (`raw & 2`) = null arena tag → effective arena = 0
- bit 0 (`raw & 1`) = indirection flag → effective = `*(untagged)`
- untag base = `raw & ~3`

Наш код:
```cpp
auto untag_msg_arena = [](std::uint8_t* msg) -> std::uint64_t {
    const auto raw = *reinterpret_cast<std::uint64_t*>(msg + 8);
    if (raw & 2) return 0;
    auto untagged = raw & ~std::uint64_t{3};
    if (raw & 1) untagged = *reinterpret_cast<std::uint64_t*>(untagged);
    return untagged;
};
```

### 4. Cross-arena copy

Когда step и qangle аллоцированы в разных аренах, FVA (`sub_7FFBE8101CC0`) делает:
1. `new_qangle = qangle.vtable[+0x10](qangle, target_arena)` (protobuf `New(Arena*)`)
2. `new_qangle.vtable[+0x30](new_qangle, qangle)` (`MergeFrom(other)`)
3. Использовать `new_qangle` вместо оригинала

Слоты `+0x10` и `+0x30` — стандартные для generated protobuf message vtables (`New` и `MergeFrom` соответственно).

### 5. `tier0!g_pMemAlloc` — общий allocator

FVA `sub_7FFBE80DC5A8` (наш `cs2_alloc`) резолвит через XOR-decoded строки:
- Module hash → `tier0.dll`
- Export → `g_pMemAlloc` (единственный публичный CS2 memory-manager)

Наш port просто использует `GetProcAddress(tier0, "g_pMemAlloc")` — эффект идентичен, обфускация обходится тем что мы легитимный DLL а не VMP-защищённый.

Vtable slots:
- `[+0x08]` = `Alloc(size)` (slot 1)
- `[+0x18]` = `Free(ptr)` (slot 3)
- Дают совместимость с CS2's protobuf runtime (обе стороны используют один allocator → нет UAF при destruct)

---

## Multi-depot support

Файл `src/version_manifest.h` содержит `#if FVA_TARGET_DEPOT == ...` блоки для каждой поддерживаемой сборки:

| Depot buildid | client.dll md5 | Size | Дата |
|---|---|---|---|
| **14167** | b0fd08261be12cceb8e5636500e5a1d0 | 37 272 728 B | 2026-07-10 (baseline) |
| **24134959** | 16917fce6c15715434f6604e8086d38a | 37 419 160 B | 2026-07-10 (current) |

RVA drift 14167 → 24134959:
```
Hook A CreateMove:      +0x3A598  (0xACEF90  → 0xB09528)
Hook B LevelInit:       +0x3C650  (0xAFDFB0  → 0xB3A600)
Hook C Serialize:       +0x23BB0  (0x1189930 → 0x11AD4E0)
CBaseUserCmdPB::New:    +0x30120  (0x4B21E0  → 0x4E2300)
CSubtickMoveStep::New:  +0x30120  (0x4B22D0  → 0x4E23F0)
CInButtonStatePB::New:  +0x30120  (0x4B2250  → 0x4E2370)
CMsgQAngle::New:        -0x28F00  (0x6840F0  → 0x65B1F0)
CSGOInputHistoryEntry:  +0x34CE0  (0x742730  → 0x777410)
CBaseUserCmdPB vtable:  +0x3FE40  (0x1995748 → 0x19D5588)
ArenaStringPtr::Set:    +0x23BB0  (0x11712C0 → 0x1194E70)
```
