`source/dlls/fva_devirt/src/main.cpp`:

```cpp
// ============================================================================
// fva_devirt — standalone static analyzer for VMProtect-obfuscated CS2 DLLs.
//
// Input:  a VMP-protected PE (typically FuckVacAgain.dll).
// Output: fva_devirt_data.json + fva_devirt_report.md next to the input file.
//
// Reproduces (without IDA) the invariants documented in
//   C:\vmp\notes\FVA_PROTECTION_STATE.md
//   C:\vmp\notes\DRAFT_VM_PAYLOAD_CORRELATION_REVIEW.md
// so the analysis is replayable on any future FVA build or similar VMP payload.
//
// What it produces:
//   - Payload identification (SHA-256, image base, section map)
//   - VMP version pinning (3.5.1 vs 3.6+) from AntiDebug primitive counts
//   - Universal VMExit tail localization (must be unique for a clean VMP wrapper)
//   - Dispatcher site inventory (jmp r10 / jmp r9 / jmp table)
//   - Per-dispatch-site preamble extraction (raw bytes, 32 preceding)
//   - AntiDebug primitive counts (int 2D / syscall / rdtsc / int 3)
//   - Routing table detection with A/B/A CRC-integrity triple classification
//
// What it does NOT produce (yet, honestly documented in the report):
//   - Decoded p-code stream (needs entry_cryptor initial key recovery)
//   - Symbolic lift of handler bodies (needs full x86 decoder + IR)
//   - Compilable source-equivalent output (needs everything above)
//
// Build:   msbuild fva_devirt.vcxproj /p:Configuration=Release /p:Platform=x64
// Usage:   fva_devirt.exe <path\to\vmp-protected.dll> [--out <dir>]
// ============================================================================

#include <windows.h>
#include <bcrypt.h>
#include <cstdio>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <map>
#include <set>
#include <algorithm>
#include <memory>

#pragma comment(lib, "bcrypt.lib")

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Utility
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

static void die(const char* fmt, ...) {
    va_list ap; va_start(ap, fmt);
    std::fprintf(stderr, "[-] "); std::vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap); std::fprintf(stderr, "\n");
    va_end(ap);
    std::exit(1);
}
static void info(const char* fmt, ...) {
    va_list ap; va_start(ap, fmt);
    std::fprintf(stdout, "[*] "); std::vfprintf(stdout, fmt, ap); std::fprintf(stdout, "\n");
    va_end(ap);
}
static void ok(const char* fmt, ...) {
    va_list ap; va_start(ap, fmt);
    std::fprintf(stdout, "[+] "); std::vfprintf(stdout, fmt, ap); std::fprintf(stdout, "\n");
    va_end(ap);
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File mapping (raw, no LoadLibrary — DLL is data, not code)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

struct Mapping {
    HANDLE file       = INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE;
    HANDLE mapping    = nullptr;
    const uint8_t* data = nullptr;
    size_t size       = 0;

    ~Mapping() {
        if (data)    UnmapViewOfFile(data);
        if (mapping) CloseHandle(mapping);
        if (file != INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) CloseHandle(file);
    }
};

static bool map_file(const char* path, Mapping& out) {
    out.file = CreateFileA(path, GENERIC_READ, FILE_SHARE_READ, nullptr,
                           OPEN_EXISTING, FILE_ATTRIBUTE_NORMAL, nullptr);
    if (out.file == INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) return false;

    LARGE_INTEGER sz;
    if (!GetFileSizeEx(out.file, &sz)) return false;
    out.size = (size_t)sz.QuadPart;

    out.mapping = CreateFileMappingA(out.file, nullptr, PAGE_READONLY, 0, 0, nullptr);
    if (!out.mapping) return false;

    out.data = (const uint8_t*)MapViewOfFile(out.mapping, FILE_MAP_READ, 0, 0, 0);
    return out.data != nullptr;
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// SHA-256 via BCrypt
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

static std::string sha256_hex(const uint8_t* data, size_t size) {
    BCRYPT_ALG_HANDLE alg = nullptr;
    BCRYPT_HASH_HANDLE h  = nullptr;
    if (BCryptOpenAlgorithmProvider(&alg, BCRYPT_SHA256_ALGORITHM, nullptr, 0) != 0) return "";
    if (BCryptCreateHash(alg, &h, nullptr, 0, nullptr, 0, 0) != 0) { BCryptCloseAlgorithmProvider(alg, 0); return ""; }
    BCryptHashData(h, (PUCHAR)data, (ULONG)size, 0);
    uint8_t digest[32] = {};
    BCryptFinishHash(h, digest, sizeof(digest), 0);
    BCryptDestroyHash(h);
    BCryptCloseAlgorithmProvider(alg, 0);
    char hex[65] = {};
    for (int i = 0; i < 32; ++i) std::snprintf(hex + i*2, 3, "%02x", digest[i]);
    return hex;
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// PE parsing
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

struct Section {
    std::string name;
    uint64_t    va_start;
    uint64_t    va_end;
    uint32_t    size;
    uint32_t    raw_off;
    uint32_t    raw_size;
    uint32_t    characteristics;
    bool        rx;
    bool        r;
    bool        rw;
    // Sanitized name: replace non-printables with '_' (VMP renames sections
    // with bytes like `.#I5` where '#' is really 0x00 or similar).
    std::string display_name;
};

struct Pe {
    uint64_t             image_base;
    uint32_t             image_size;
    bool                 is_pe32_plus;
    std::vector<Section> sections;
    uint32_t             entry_rva;
    std::vector<uint32_t> tls_callback_rvas;
};

static std::string sanitize_name(const char* raw, size_t maxlen) {
    std::string s;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < maxlen && raw[i]; ++i) {
        unsigned char c = (unsigned char)raw[i];
        s += (c >= 32 && c < 127) ? (char)c : '_';
    }
    return s;
}

static bool parse_pe(const Mapping& m, Pe& pe) {
    if (m.size < 0x40) return false;
    const IMAGE_DOS_HEADER* dos = (const IMAGE_DOS_HEADER*)m.data;
    if (dos->e_magic != IMAGE_DOS_SIGNATURE) return false;
    if ((uint32_t)dos->e_lfanew + sizeof(IMAGE_NT_HEADERS64) > m.size) return false;

    const IMAGE_NT_HEADERS64* nt = (const IMAGE_NT_HEADERS64*)(m.data + dos->e_lfanew);
    if (nt->Signature != IMAGE_NT_SIGNATURE) return false;
    if (nt->OptionalHeader.Magic != IMAGE_NT_OPTIONAL_HDR64_MAGIC) {
        std::fprintf(stderr, "[-] Not PE32+; only x64 supported.\n");
        return false;
    }

    pe.image_base   = nt->OptionalHeader.ImageBase;
    pe.image_size   = nt->OptionalHeader.SizeOfImage;
    pe.is_pe32_plus = true;
    pe.entry_rva    = nt->OptionalHeader.AddressOfEntryPoint;

    // TLS callbacks (best-effort — read TLS directory)
    const IMAGE_DATA_DIRECTORY& tls_dir = nt->OptionalHeader.DataDirectory[IMAGE_DIRECTORY_ENTRY_TLS];
    // (we resolve callbacks only after we have sections mapped; deferred)

    const IMAGE_SECTION_HEADER* sec = (const IMAGE_SECTION_HEADER*)(
        (const uint8_t*)&nt->OptionalHeader + nt->FileHeader.SizeOfOptionalHeader);
    for (uint16_t i = 0; i < nt->FileHeader.NumberOfSections; ++i) {
        const IMAGE_SECTION_HEADER& s = sec[i];
        Section S;
        S.name            = std::string((const char*)s.Name, strnlen((const char*)s.Name, 8));
        S.display_name    = sanitize_name((const char*)s.Name, 8);
        S.va_start        = pe.image_base + s.VirtualAddress;
        S.va_end          = S.va_start + s.Misc.VirtualSize;
        S.size            = s.Misc.VirtualSize;
        S.raw_off         = s.PointerToRawData;
        S.raw_size        = s.SizeOfRawData;
        S.characteristics = s.Characteristics;
        S.rx              = (s.Characteristics & IMAGE_SCN_MEM_EXECUTE) != 0;
        S.r               = (s.Characteristics & IMAGE_SCN_MEM_READ)    != 0;
        S.rw              = (s.Characteristics & IMAGE_SCN_MEM_WRITE)   != 0;
        pe.sections.push_back(S);
    }

    // TLS callbacks: TLS directory holds a VA to the AddressOfCallBacks array;
    // walk it to zero-terminator.
    if (tls_dir.Size >= sizeof(IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64) && tls_dir.VirtualAddress) {
        // Find raw offset of TLS dir.
        auto rva_to_raw = [&](uint32_t rva) -> uint32_t {
            for (const auto& s : pe.sections) {
                uint32_t sec_rva = (uint32_t)(s.va_start - pe.image_base);
                if (rva >= sec_rva && rva < sec_rva + s.size) {
                    return s.raw_off + (rva - sec_rva);
                }
            }
            return 0;
        };
        uint32_t tls_raw = rva_to_raw(tls_dir.VirtualAddress);
        if (tls_raw && tls_raw + sizeof(IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64) <= m.size) {
            const IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64* tls = (const IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64*)(m.data + tls_raw);
            uint64_t cb_va = tls->AddressOfCallBacks;
            if (cb_va >= pe.image_base) {
                uint32_t cb_rva = (uint32_t)(cb_va - pe.image_base);
                uint32_t cb_raw = rva_to_raw(cb_rva);
                if (cb_raw) {
                    for (int i = 0; i < 32; ++i) {
                        if (cb_raw + (i+1)*sizeof(uint64_t) > m.size) break;
                        uint64_t v = *(const uint64_t*)(m.data + cb_raw + i*sizeof(uint64_t));
                        if (!v) break;
                        if (v >= pe.image_base) pe.tls_callback_rvas.push_back((uint32_t)(v - pe.image_base));
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return true;
}

// Given a VA inside a section, return pointer into the mapping (or nullptr).
static const uint8_t* va_to_ptr(const Mapping& m, const Pe& pe, uint64_t va) {
    for (const auto& s : pe.sections) {
        if (va >= s.va_start && va < s.va_end) {
            uint32_t off = (uint32_t)(va - s.va_start);
            if (s.raw_off + off < m.size) return m.data + s.raw_off + off;
            return nullptr;
        }
    }
    return nullptr;
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Byte-pattern scanner with '??' wildcards. Pattern format: hex bytes,
// space-separated, '??' for any byte. Case-insensitive.
// Returns absolute VAs of matches, scoped to a single section.
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

struct Pattern {
    std::vector<uint8_t> bytes;
    std::vector<bool>    mask; // true = must match, false = wildcard
};

static bool parse_pattern(const char* s, Pattern& p) {
    p.bytes.clear(); p.mask.clear();
    while (*s) {
        while (*s == ' ' || *s == '\t') ++s;
        if (!*s) break;
        if (s[0] == '?' && s[1] == '?') { p.bytes.push_back(0); p.mask.push_back(false); s += 2; continue; }
        auto hex = [](char c) -> int {
            if (c >= '0' && c <= '9') return c - '0';
            if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f') return 10 + c - 'a';
            if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F') return 10 + c - 'A';
            return -1;
        };
        int hi = hex(s[0]), lo = hex(s[1]);
        if (hi < 0 || lo < 0) return false;
        p.bytes.push_back((uint8_t)((hi << 4) | lo));
        p.mask.push_back(true);
        s += 2;
    }
    return !p.bytes.empty();
}

static std::vector<uint64_t> scan_section(const Mapping& m, const Pe& pe,
                                          const Section& s, const Pattern& p,
                                          size_t limit = SIZE_MAX)
{
    std::vector<uint64_t> hits;
    if (s.raw_off + s.raw_size > m.size) return hits;
    const uint8_t* base = m.data + s.raw_off;
    size_t sec_size = (size_t)std::min<uint32_t>(s.raw_size, s.size);
    if (p.bytes.size() > sec_size) return hits;
    size_t end = sec_size - p.bytes.size() + 1;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < end; ++i) {
        bool match = true;
        for (size_t j = 0; j < p.bytes.size(); ++j) {
            if (p.mask[j] && base[i + j] != p.bytes[j]) { match = false; break; }
        }
        if (match) {
            hits.push_back(s.va_start + i);
            if (hits.size() >= limit) break;
        }
    }
    return hits;
}

static std::vector<uint64_t> scan_all_rx(const Mapping& m, const Pe& pe,
                                         const char* pat_str, size_t limit = SIZE_MAX)
{
    Pattern p;
    if (!parse_pattern(pat_str, p)) return {};
    std::vector<uint64_t> hits;
    for (const auto& s : pe.sections) {
        if (!s.rx) continue;
        auto h = scan_section(m, pe, s, p, limit);
        hits.insert(hits.end(), h.begin(), h.end());
        if (hits.size() >= limit) break;
    }
    return hits;
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Preamble extraction: for each site, read N preceding raw bytes.
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

static std::string bytes_hex(const uint8_t* p, size_t n) {
    std::string s;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        char buf[4];
        std::snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "%02X ", p[i]);
        s += buf;
    }
    if (!s.empty()) s.pop_back();
    return s;
}

struct Preamble {
    uint64_t site_va;
    std::string preceding_hex; // 32 bytes before the site (or fewer if section start)
    std::string at_hex;        // 8 bytes at the site
};

static std::vector<Preamble> extract_preambles(const Mapping& m, const Pe& pe,
                                                const std::vector<uint64_t>& sites,
                                                size_t before = 32, size_t at = 8)
{
    std::vector<Preamble> out;
    out.reserve(sites.size());
    for (uint64_t site : sites) {
        Preamble pr; pr.site_va = site;
        for (const auto& s : pe.sections) {
            if (site >= s.va_start && site < s.va_end) {
                uint64_t start = site >= s.va_start + before ? site - before : s.va_start;
                size_t   pre_n = (size_t)(site - start);
                const uint8_t* pre_p = va_to_ptr(m, pe, start);
                if (pre_p) pr.preceding_hex = bytes_hex(pre_p, pre_n);
                const uint8_t* at_p = va_to_ptr(m, pe, site);
                if (at_p && site + at <= s.va_end) pr.at_hex = bytes_hex(at_p, at);
                break;
            }
        }
        out.push_back(pr);
    }
    return out;
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Routing table detection.
// Scan the mapping for aligned 4-byte-value arrays where each value is a
// plausible RVA (in [0x1000, image_size)) and the array is >= 8 entries.
// Classify entries by containing section. Report A/B/A triple pattern hits.
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

struct RoutingTable {
    uint64_t va;
    size_t   entry_count;
    std::vector<uint32_t> entries;
    std::vector<std::string> entry_sections;
    size_t   aba_triples; // count of positions where entry[i] == entry[i+2] within window
};

static const Section* section_for_rva(const Pe& pe, uint32_t rva) {
    uint64_t va = pe.image_base + rva;
    for (const auto& s : pe.sections) {
        if (va >= s.va_start && va < s.va_end) return &s;
    }
    return nullptr;
}

static std::vector<RoutingTable> detect_routing_tables(const Mapping& m, const Pe& pe,
                                                       size_t min_entries = 12)
{
    std::vector<RoutingTable> tables;
    for (const auto& sec : pe.sections) {
        if (!sec.rx && !sec.r) continue; // routing tables live in R/RX
        if (sec.raw_off + sec.raw_size > m.size) continue;
        const uint8_t* base = m.data + sec.raw_off;
        size_t sec_size = std::min<size_t>(sec.raw_size, sec.size);
        // Slide a window looking for the start of a plausible table.
        size_t i = 0;
        while (i + 4 <= sec_size) {
            // Check candidate table start: at least min_entries consecutive
            // 4-byte values that look like RVAs.
            size_t j = i;
            std::vector<uint32_t> entries;
            while (j + 4 <= sec_size) {
                uint32_t v = *(const uint32_t*)(base + j);
                if (v < 0x1000 || v >= pe.image_size) break;
                entries.push_back(v);
                j += 4;
                if (entries.size() >= 4096) break; // safety
            }
            if (entries.size() >= min_entries) {
                RoutingTable rt;
                rt.va = sec.va_start + i;
                rt.entries = entries;
                rt.entry_count = entries.size();
                rt.aba_triples = 0;
                rt.entry_sections.reserve(entries.size());
                for (uint32_t rva : entries) {
                    const Section* ts = section_for_rva(pe, rva);
                    rt.entry_sections.push_back(ts ? ts->display_name : "?");
                }
                // Count A/B/A triples.
                for (size_t k = 0; k + 3 <= entries.size(); ++k) {
                    if (entries[k] == entries[k + 2] && entries[k] != entries[k + 1])
                        rt.aba_triples++;
                }
                tables.push_back(std::move(rt));
                i = j; // skip past
            } else {
                i += 4;
            }
        }
    }
    return tables;
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Report emission
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

static std::string escape_json(const std::string& in) {
    std::string s;
    for (char c : in) {
        switch (c) {
            case '"':  s += "\\\""; break;
            case '\\': s += "\\\\"; break;
            case '\n': s += "\\n";  break;
            case '\r': s += "\\r";  break;
            case '\t': s += "\\t";  break;
            default:
                if ((unsigned char)c < 0x20) {
                    char b[8]; std::snprintf(b, sizeof(b), "\\u%04x", (unsigned char)c);
                    s += b;
                } else s += c;
        }
    }
    return s;
}

struct Report {
    std::string          input_path;
    std::string          sha256;
    size_t               file_size;
    uint64_t             image_base;
    uint32_t             image_size;
    std::vector<Section> sections;
    uint32_t             entry_rva;
    std::vector<uint32_t> tls_callback_rvas;

    std::vector<uint64_t> vmexit_tail_sites;
    std::vector<uint64_t> jmp_r10_sites;
    std::vector<uint64_t> jmp_r9_sites;
    std::vector<uint64_t> jmp_table_sites;
    std::vector<uint64_t> int_2d_sites;
    std::vector<uint64_t> syscall_sites;
    std::vector<uint64_t> rdtsc_sites;
    std::vector<uint64_t> int_3_sites;

    std::vector<Preamble>     jmp_r10_preambles;
    std::vector<Preamble>     jmp_r9_preambles;
    std::vector<RoutingTable> routing_tables;

    std::string vmp_version_verdict;
    std::string vmp_version_reasoning;
};

static void write_json(const Report& r, const std::string& path) {
    FILE* f = std::fopen(path.c_str(), "wb");
    if (!f) die("cannot open %s for write", path.c_str());
    std::fprintf(f, "{\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"input_path\": \"%s\",\n", escape_json(r.input_path).c_str());
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"sha256\": \"%s\",\n",     r.sha256.c_str());
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"file_size\": %zu,\n",     r.file_size);
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"image_base\": \"0x%llx\",\n", (unsigned long long)r.image_base);
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"image_size\": \"0x%x\",\n",   r.image_size);
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"entry_rva\":  \"0x%x\",\n",   r.entry_rva);
    // sections
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"sections\": [\n");
    for (size_t i = 0; i < r.sections.size(); ++i) {
        const auto& s = r.sections[i];
        std::fprintf(f, "    { \"name\": \"%s\", \"va_start\": \"0x%llx\", \"va_end\": \"0x%llx\", \"size\": \"0x%x\", \"perms\": \"%s%s%s\" }%s\n",
            s.display_name.c_str(), (unsigned long long)s.va_start, (unsigned long long)s.va_end,
            s.size, s.r ? "r" : "-", s.rw ? "w" : "-", s.rx ? "x" : "-",
            (i + 1 == r.sections.size()) ? "" : ",");
    }
    std::fprintf(f, "  ],\n");
    // TLS callbacks
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"tls_callbacks\": [");
    for (size_t i = 0; i < r.tls_callback_rvas.size(); ++i)
        std::fprintf(f, "%s\"0x%x\"", i ? ", " : "", r.tls_callback_rvas[i]);
    std::fprintf(f, "],\n");
    // sites
    auto dump_sites = [&](const char* key, const std::vector<uint64_t>& v, bool last) {
        std::fprintf(f, "  \"%s\": {\n    \"count\": %zu,\n    \"sites\": [", key, v.size());
        for (size_t i = 0; i < v.size() && i < 200; ++i)
            std::fprintf(f, "%s\"0x%llx\"", i ? ", " : "", (unsigned long long)v[i]);
        if (v.size() > 200) std::fprintf(f, ", \"...(truncated)\"");
        std::fprintf(f, "]\n  }%s\n", last ? "" : ",");
    };
    dump_sites("vmexit_tail_add_rsp_138",   r.vmexit_tail_sites, false);
    dump_sites("dispatcher_jmp_r10",        r.jmp_r10_sites,     false);
    dump_sites("dispatcher_jmp_r9",         r.jmp_r9_sites,      false);
    dump_sites("dispatcher_jmp_table_rcx8", r.jmp_table_sites,   false);
    dump_sites("antidebug_int_2d",          r.int_2d_sites,      false);
    dump_sites("antidebug_syscall",         r.syscall_sites,     false);
    dump_sites("antidebug_rdtsc",           r.rdtsc_sites,       false);
    dump_sites("antidebug_int_3",           r.int_3_sites,       false);

    // preambles
    auto dump_pre = [&](const char* key, const std::vector<Preamble>& v, bool last) {
        std::fprintf(f, "  \"%s\": [\n", key);
        for (size_t i = 0; i < v.size() && i < 200; ++i) {
            std::fprintf(f, "    { \"site\": \"0x%llx\", \"pre\": \"%s\", \"at\": \"%s\" }%s\n",
                (unsigned long long)v[i].site_va,
                v[i].preceding_hex.c_str(), v[i].at_hex.c_str(),
                (i + 1 == std::min<size_t>(v.size(), 200)) ? "" : ",");
        }
        std::fprintf(f, "  ]%s\n", last ? "" : ",");
    };
    dump_pre("preambles_jmp_r10", r.jmp_r10_preambles, false);
    dump_pre("preambles_jmp_r9",  r.jmp_r9_preambles,  false);

    // routing tables
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"routing_tables\": [\n");
    for (size_t i = 0; i < r.routing_tables.size(); ++i) {
        const auto& t = r.routing_tables[i];
        std::fprintf(f, "    { \"va\": \"0x%llx\", \"entries\": %zu, \"aba_triples\": %zu, \"first_10\": [",
            (unsigned long long)t.va, t.entry_count, t.aba_triples);
        size_t n = std::min<size_t>(10, t.entries.size());
        for (size_t j = 0; j < n; ++j) {
            std::fprintf(f, "%s{ \"rva\": \"0x%x\", \"sec\": \"%s\" }",
                j ? ", " : "", t.entries[j],
                j < t.entry_sections.size() ? t.entry_sections[j].c_str() : "?");
        }
        std::fprintf(f, "] }%s\n", (i + 1 == r.routing_tables.size()) ? "" : ",");
    }
    std::fprintf(f, "  ],\n");

    // verdict
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"vmp_version_verdict\": \"%s\",\n", escape_json(r.vmp_version_verdict).c_str());
    std::fprintf(f, "  \"vmp_version_reasoning\": \"%s\"\n", escape_json(r.vmp_version_reasoning).c_str());
    std::fprintf(f, "}\n");
    std::fclose(f);
}

static void write_markdown(const Report& r, const std::string& path) {
    FILE* f = std::fopen(path.c_str(), "wb");
    if (!f) die("cannot open %s for write", path.c_str());

    std::fprintf(f, "# fva_devirt report\n\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "**Input:** `%s`\n", r.input_path.c_str());
    std::fprintf(f, "**SHA-256:** `%s`\n", r.sha256.c_str());
    std::fprintf(f, "**File size:** %zu B\n", r.file_size);
    std::fprintf(f, "**Image base:** `0x%llx`\n", (unsigned long long)r.image_base);
    std::fprintf(f, "**Image size:** `0x%x`\n", r.image_size);
    std::fprintf(f, "**Entry RVA:** `0x%x`\n\n", r.entry_rva);

    std::fprintf(f, "## Sections\n\n| # | Name | VA range | Size | Perms |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n");
    for (size_t i = 0; i < r.sections.size(); ++i) {
        const auto& s = r.sections[i];
        std::fprintf(f, "| %zu | `%s` | `0x%llx..0x%llx` | `0x%x` | %s%s%s |\n",
            i, s.display_name.c_str(),
            (unsigned long long)s.va_start, (unsigned long long)s.va_end,
            s.size, s.r ? "r" : "-", s.rw ? "w" : "-", s.rx ? "x" : "-");
    }
    std::fprintf(f, "\n");

    std::fprintf(f, "## VMP version verdict\n\n**%s**\n\n%s\n\n", r.vmp_version_verdict.c_str(), r.vmp_version_reasoning.c_str());

    std::fprintf(f, "## VMExit universal tail\n\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "Pattern: `48 81 C4 38 01 00 00 C3` (`add rsp, 138h ; retn`)\n\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "**Hits: %zu**", r.vmexit_tail_sites.size());
    if (r.vmexit_tail_sites.size() == 1) {
        std::fprintf(f, " — unique, consistent with VMP wrapper (single funnel-point for all VM segment returns).\n\n");
        std::fprintf(f, "Site: `0x%llx`.  **Context frame size = 0x138 (312 bytes)**.\n\n", (unsigned long long)r.vmexit_tail_sites[0]);
    } else {
        std::fprintf(f, ".\n\n");
    }

    auto section_of = [&](uint64_t va) -> std::string {
        for (const auto& s : r.sections) {
            if (va >= s.va_start && va < s.va_end) return s.display_name;
        }
        return "?";
    };

    std::fprintf(f, "## Dispatcher sites\n\n| Pattern | Instruction | Count |\n|---|---|---|\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "| `41 FF E2` | `jmp r10` | %zu |\n",   r.jmp_r10_sites.size());
    std::fprintf(f, "| `41 FF E1` | `jmp r9`  | %zu |\n",   r.jmp_r9_sites.size());
    std::fprintf(f, "| `FF 24 C1` | `jmp qword ptr [rcx*8+K]` | %zu |\n\n", r.jmp_table_sites.size());

    std::fprintf(f, "## AntiDebug primitives\n\n| Bytes | Instruction | Hits | 3.6+ signal? |\n|---|---|---|---|\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "| `CD 2D` | `int 2Dh`  | %zu | **yes** (absent in 3.5.1 sources) |\n", r.int_2d_sites.size());
    std::fprintf(f, "| `0F 05` | `syscall`  | %zu | **yes** (direct-syscall bypass of usermode hooks) |\n", r.syscall_sites.size());
    std::fprintf(f, "| `0F 31` | `rdtsc`    | %zu | timing check (present in all versions but heavy usage = AntiDebug) |\n", r.rdtsc_sites.size());
    std::fprintf(f, "| `CD 03` | `int 3`    | %zu | SEH-guarded BP probes |\n\n", r.int_3_sites.size());

    std::fprintf(f, "## Sample dispatch preambles (first 16 `jmp r10`)\n\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "Each line shows 32 bytes immediately before the dispatcher site + 8 bytes at the site.\n\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "| Site | Section | 32 preceding bytes | at site |\n|---|---|---|---|\n");
    for (size_t i = 0; i < r.jmp_r10_preambles.size() && i < 16; ++i) {
        const auto& p = r.jmp_r10_preambles[i];
        std::fprintf(f, "| `0x%llx` | `%s` | `%s` | `%s` |\n",
            (unsigned long long)p.site_va, section_of(p.site_va).c_str(),
            p.preceding_hex.c_str(), p.at_hex.c_str());
    }
    if (r.jmp_r10_preambles.size() > 16)
        std::fprintf(f, "\n_(%zu more sites in fva_devirt_data.json)_\n", r.jmp_r10_preambles.size() - 16);
    std::fprintf(f, "\n");

    std::fprintf(f, "## Routing tables (candidate)\n\n");
    if (r.routing_tables.empty()) {
        std::fprintf(f, "_No RVA-like tables detected with min-entries=12._\n\n");
    } else {
        std::fprintf(f, "| # | VA | Entries | A/B/A triples | First 10 entries |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n");
        for (size_t i = 0; i < r.routing_tables.size() && i < 32; ++i) {
            const auto& t = r.routing_tables[i];
            std::string first10;
            for (size_t j = 0; j < 10 && j < t.entries.size(); ++j) {
                char buf[32];
                std::snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "0x%x%s(%s) ",
                    t.entries[j], j + 1 == 10 ? "" : " ",
                    j < t.entry_sections.size() ? t.entry_sections[j].c_str() : "?");
                first10 += buf;
            }
            std::fprintf(f, "| %zu | `0x%llx` | %zu | %zu | %s |\n",
                i, (unsigned long long)t.va, t.entry_count, t.aba_triples, first10.c_str());
        }
    }
    std::fprintf(f, "\n");

    std::fprintf(f, "## What this report does NOT prove\n\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "- **Decoded p-code stream** — requires `entry_cryptor` initial key recovery (per-build fixup in the entry stub). Not implemented in this tool.\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "- **Handler-body symbolic lift** — requires an x86 decoder + IR + full CFG walk of each VM segment. Deferred; the preamble bytes above are the raw input to such a lifter.\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "- **Compilable source-equivalent output** — requires the two above plus a code generator. Out of scope for a single-session tool.\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "For the full analysis methodology and next static steps, see:\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "- `C:\\vmp\\notes\\FVA_PROTECTION_STATE.md`\n");
    std::fprintf(f, "- `C:\\vmp\\notes\\DRAFT_VM_PAYLOAD_CORRELATION_REVIEW.md`\n");

    std::fclose(f);
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Version pinning
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

static void pin_vmp_version(Report& r) {
    // Heuristic:
    //   VMP 3.6+   : int_2d >= 10 AND syscall >= 15  (AntiDebug enabled)
    //   VMP 3.5.1 : int_2d < 3 AND syscall < 5      (AntiDebug absent by default)
    //   uncertain : between
    size_t i2d  = r.int_2d_sites.size();
    size_t sc   = r.syscall_sites.size();
    size_t rdt  = r.rdtsc_sites.size();

    char buf[512];
    if (i2d >= 10 && sc >= 15) {
        r.vmp_version_verdict = "VMP 3.6+";
        std::snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),
            "int 2D hits=%zu, syscall hits=%zu, rdtsc hits=%zu. The `int 2Dh` instruction is not emitted by VMProtect 3.5.1 runtime; its presence in double-digit counts, together with heavy direct-syscall usage, is a canonical 3.6+ AntiDebug signature.",
            i2d, sc, rdt);
    } else if (i2d < 3 && sc < 5) {
        r.vmp_version_verdict = "VMP 3.5.1 (or older)";
        std::snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),
            "int 2D hits=%zu, syscall hits=%zu — below the AntiDebug threshold. Consistent with a 3.5.x build without the 3.6 AntiDebug catalog.",
            i2d, sc);
    } else {
        r.vmp_version_verdict = "uncertain";
        std::snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),
            "int 2D hits=%zu, syscall hits=%zu — mixed signal. Could be a private 3.5.x fork with partial AntiDebug enabled, or a stripped 3.6 build.",
            i2d, sc);
    }
    r.vmp_version_reasoning = buf;
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Main
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

static void usage() {
    std::fprintf(stderr,
        "fva_devirt <path_to_dll> [--out <dir>]\n"
        "  Produces <basename>_devirt_data.json and <basename>_devirt_report.md\n"
        "  next to the input, or in <dir> if --out is given.\n");
    std::exit(1);
}

int main(int argc, char** argv) {
    const char* input   = nullptr;
    const char* out_dir = nullptr;
    for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {
        if (std::strcmp(argv[i], "--out") == 0 && i + 1 < argc) out_dir = argv[++i];
        else if (!input) input = argv[i];
        else usage();
    }
    if (!input) usage();

    info("mapping %s", input);
    Mapping m;
    if (!map_file(input, m)) die("cannot map input file");
    ok("mapped %zu bytes", m.size);

    Pe pe;
    if (!parse_pe(m, pe)) die("PE parse failed (not PE32+ or malformed)");
    ok("PE parsed: image_base=0x%llx image_size=0x%x sections=%zu",
       (unsigned long long)pe.image_base, pe.image_size, pe.sections.size());

    Report r;
    r.input_path = input;
    r.sha256     = sha256_hex(m.data, m.size);
    r.file_size  = m.size;
    r.image_base = pe.image_base;
    r.image_size = pe.image_size;
    r.entry_rva  = pe.entry_rva;
    r.sections   = pe.sections;
    r.tls_callback_rvas = pe.tls_callback_rvas;

    info("scanning for VMExit universal tail (48 81 C4 38 01 00 00 C3)");
    r.vmexit_tail_sites = scan_all_rx(m, pe, "48 81 C4 38 01 00 00 C3");
    ok("VMExit tail: %zu hit%s", r.vmexit_tail_sites.size(), r.vmexit_tail_sites.size() == 1 ? " (unique — good)" : "s");

    info("scanning for dispatcher sites");
    r.jmp_r10_sites   = scan_all_rx(m, pe, "41 FF E2");
    r.jmp_r9_sites    = scan_all_rx(m, pe, "41 FF E1");
    r.jmp_table_sites = scan_all_rx(m, pe, "FF 24 C1");
    ok("jmp r10=%zu  jmp r9=%zu  jmp [rcx*8+K]=%zu",
       r.jmp_r10_sites.size(), r.jmp_r9_sites.size(), r.jmp_table_sites.size());

    info("scanning for AntiDebug primitives");
    r.int_2d_sites  = scan_all_rx(m, pe, "CD 2D");
    r.syscall_sites = scan_all_rx(m, pe, "0F 05");
    r.rdtsc_sites   = scan_all_rx(m, pe, "0F 31");
    r.int_3_sites   = scan_all_rx(m, pe, "CD 03");
    ok("int 2D=%zu  syscall=%zu  rdtsc=%zu  int 3=%zu",
       r.int_2d_sites.size(), r.syscall_sites.size(), r.rdtsc_sites.size(), r.int_3_sites.size());

    info("extracting preambles (32 bytes before each dispatch site)");
    r.jmp_r10_preambles = extract_preambles(m, pe, r.jmp_r10_sites);
    r.jmp_r9_preambles  = extract_preambles(m, pe, r.jmp_r9_sites);
    ok("extracted %zu r10 + %zu r9 preambles",
       r.jmp_r10_preambles.size(), r.jmp_r9_preambles.size());

    info("detecting routing tables");
    r.routing_tables = detect_routing_tables(m, pe);
    ok("candidate routing tables: %zu", r.routing_tables.size());

    pin_vmp_version(r);
    ok("VMP verdict: %s", r.vmp_version_verdict.c_str());

    // Determine output paths.
    std::string base = input;
    size_t sl = base.find_last_of("/\\");
    std::string dir = (sl != std::string::npos) ? base.substr(0, sl) : ".";
    std::string leaf = (sl != std::string::npos) ? base.substr(sl + 1) : base;
    size_t dot = leaf.find_last_of('.');
    std::string stem = (dot != std::string::npos) ? leaf.substr(0, dot) : leaf;
    if (out_dir) dir = out_dir;

    std::string json_path = dir + "\\" + stem + "_devirt_data.json";
    std::string md_path   = dir + "\\" + stem + "_devirt_report.md";

    info("writing %s", json_path.c_str());
    write_json(r, json_path);
    info("writing %s", md_path.c_str());
    write_markdown(r, md_path);

    ok("done.");
    return 0;
}

```

`source/drivers/CS2HexSyncCompatDriver/README.md`:

```md
# CS2HexSyncCompatDriver

Kernel-mode WDM driver, реализующий **HexSync IOCTL protocol** — reverse-engineered из NLinjector(2).exe HexSyncService.sys. Служит kernel-side партнёром для [`CS2UnifiedInjector`](../../tools/CS2UnifiedInjector/) `--method kernel`.

## Что делает

Экспозит `\Device\HexSyncService` (символьная ссылка `\\.\HexSyncService`) с **9 IOCTL codes** для manipulation другого процесса **без** user-mode `OpenProcess` handle. Anti-cheat, следящий за `PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS` handles, слепой к этому пути.

## IOCTL codes (совпадают с NLinjector byte-for-byte)

| Code | Handler | В in-буфер | Из out-буфер |
|---|---|---|---|
| `0x222000` READ_MEMORY | `HandleRead` | `{pid, addr, size}` | `bytes[size]` |
| `0x222004` WRITE_MEMORY | `HandleWrite` | `{pid, addr, size, bytes[size]}` | — |
| `0x222008` GET_MODULE_BASE | `HandleGetModule` | `{pid, WCHAR name[260]}` | `{OutBase}` |
| `0x22200C` SELF_TEST | `HandleSelfTest` | — | `{0x8421, 2}` = magic `0x200008421` |
| `0x222010` GET_SECRET | `NOT_SUPPORTED` | (см. ниже) | (см. ниже) |
| `0x222014` SET_SECRET | `NOT_SUPPORTED` | (см. ниже) | (см. ниже) |
| `0x222018` ALLOCATE_MEMORY | `HandleAllocate` | `{pid, size, type, protect, addr=NULL}` | `{addr}` |
| `0x22201C` PROTECT_MEMORY | `HandleProtect` | `{pid, addr, size, newProt}` | `{oldProt}` |
| `0x222020` CREATE_THREAD | `HandleCreateThread` | `{pid, start_fn, param}` | — |

**SECRET channel не реализован** — оригинальный HexSync хранит XOR-encoded 256-byte буфер в глобалах `dword_1400051A0` / `unk_1400050A0` для хранения config между IOCTL sessions. Нашему injector'у эта функция не нужна — возвращается `STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED`.

## Kernel-side защиты

Все хендлеры используют:

1. **`PsLookupProcessByProcessId`** + `ObDereferenceObject` — сбалансированный object reference lifecycle
2. **`PsAcquireProcessExitSynchronization`** (`ExitGuard` RAII) — target не exit'ит во время работы
3. **`KeStackAttachProcess`** + `KeUnstackDetachProcess` (`Attached` RAII) — для operations нуждающихся в контексте target'а (`Zw*VirtualMemory`)
4. **SEH `__try/__except`** — вокруг всех cross-process memory operations
5. **Buffer size validation** — все IOCTL'ы проверяют `InputBufferLength >= sizeof(req)` до parse'а

## Загрузка

Три опции:

### 1. sc create + testsigning (простейший)
```powershell
bcdedit /set testsigning on   # + reboot
sc create HexSyncService type= kernel binPath= "C:\path\CS2HexSyncCompatDriver.sys"
sc start HexSyncService
```

### 2. KernelDriverMapper (наш) через iqvw64e.sys
```powershell
KernelDriverMapper.exe --key HexSyncCompat --indPages CS2HexSyncCompatDriver.sys
```

### 3. hfiref0x/KDU (65 vulnerable driver providers)
```
kdu.exe -map CS2HexSyncCompatDriver.sys
```

## Использование от injector'а

После загрузки driver'а:
```powershell
CS2UnifiedInjector.exe --method kernel --dll payload.dll --target cs2.exe --device \\.\HexSyncService
```

Injector сам делает 6 sequential IOCTL'ов:
1. `SELF_TEST` (verify magic)
2. `ALLOCATE_MEMORY` (get remote page in cs2)
3. `WRITE_MEMORY` (put DLL path)
4. `READ_MEMORY` (verify write via memcmp)
5. `GET_MODULE_BASE` (resolve remote kernel32)
6. `CREATE_THREAD` (start `LoadLibraryW(remote_path)`)

## Signed vs unsigned build

Проект собирается unsigned (`SignMode=Off`) — для `sc start` требуется testsigning. Для production подписать через WHQL или Extended Validation code-signing cert.

## Улучшения vs оригинальный HexSyncService

| Feature | Оригинальный HexSync | Наш compat |
|---|---|---|
| 9 IOCTL codes | ✅ | ✅ |
| Buffer validation | ✅ (with lots of DbgPrint) | ✅ (silent in release) |
| `PsAcquireProcessExitSynchronization` | ❌ | ✅ (KDU-style guard) |
| `KeStackAttachProcess` для alloc/protect | ✅ (unattached-mixed) | ✅ (explicit RAII scope) |
| Secret buffer (0x222010/14) | ✅ (XOR encoded) | ❌ (NOT_SUPPORTED — не нужен) |
| Debug logging в release | Verbose (все IOCTL'ы) | Silent (`#define HEXSYNC_VERBOSE 0` default) |
| Проверка WDF framework binding | ✅ (WdfVersionBind) | ❌ (raw WDM, простой) |

## См. также

- [`docs/NLINJECTOR_REVERSE.md`](../../../docs/NLINJECTOR_REVERSE.md) — полный reverse оригинала
- [`shared/stealth_mode.h`](../shared/stealth_mode.h) — общие KDU-style hardening helpers
- [`docs/DRIVER_HARDENING.md`](../../../docs/DRIVER_HARDENING.md) — roadmap улучшений

```

`source/drivers/CS2HexSyncCompatDriver/main.cpp`:

```cpp
//
// CS2HexSyncCompatDriver — kernel-mode driver implementing the HexSync IOCTL
// protocol (byte-for-byte compatible with NLinjector's HexSyncService.sys).
//
// Reversed via IDA MCP against the extracted HexSyncService.sys and rewritten
// to match its dispatch logic 1:1. Field layouts in shared.h match the original.
//
// Loaded via KernelDriverMapper (kdmap) — no signing required.
//

#include <ntifs.h>
#include <ntstrsafe.h>
#include "shared.h"

#if defined(HEXSYNC_VERBOSE) && HEXSYNC_VERBOSE
#define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)  DbgPrintEx(0x4Du, 3u, "[HexSync] " fmt "\n", ##__VA_ARGS__)
#define LOG_ERR(fmt, ...)   DbgPrintEx(0x4Du, 0u, "[HexSync] ERR " fmt "\n", ##__VA_ARGS__)
#else
#define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)  ((void)0)
#define LOG_ERR(fmt, ...)   ((void)0)
#endif

extern "C" {
    NTKERNELAPI PPEB     NTAPI PsGetProcessPeb(_In_ PEPROCESS);
    NTKERNELAPI NTSTATUS NTAPI MmCopyVirtualMemory(
        _In_ PEPROCESS FromProcess, _In_ PVOID FromAddress,
        _In_ PEPROCESS ToProcess,   _Out_ PVOID ToAddress,
        _In_ SIZE_T BufferSize, _In_ KPROCESSOR_MODE PreviousMode, _Out_ PSIZE_T ReturnSize);
    NTSTATUS NTAPI ZwProtectVirtualMemory(
        _In_ HANDLE ProcessHandle, _Inout_ PVOID* BaseAddress,
        _Inout_ PSIZE_T RegionSize, _In_ ULONG NewProtect, _Out_ PULONG OldProtect);
}

// Resolved dynamically to avoid link-time DDK requirement:
using pfnZwCreateThreadEx = NTSTATUS(NTAPI*)(
    PHANDLE, ACCESS_MASK, POBJECT_ATTRIBUTES, HANDLE, PVOID, PVOID,
    ULONG, SIZE_T, SIZE_T, SIZE_T, PVOID);
using pfnRtlCreateUserThread = NTSTATUS(NTAPI*)(
    HANDLE ProcessHandle, PSECURITY_DESCRIPTOR SecurityDescriptor,
    BOOLEAN CreateSuspended, ULONG ZeroBits, PSIZE_T MaximumStackSize,
    PSIZE_T InitialStackSize, PVOID StartAddress, PVOID Parameter,
    PHANDLE ThreadHandle, PVOID ClientId);

static pfnZwCreateThreadEx     g_ZwCreateThreadEx     = nullptr;
static pfnRtlCreateUserThread  g_RtlCreateUserThread  = nullptr;

// PEB walking types — minimal x64 layout, matches Win10..Win11 24H2.
typedef struct _PEB_LDR_DATA_S {
    ULONG      Length;
    UCHAR      Initialized;
    PVOID      SsHandle;
    LIST_ENTRY InLoadOrderModuleList;
} PEB_LDR_DATA_S, *PPEB_LDR_DATA_S;

typedef struct _LDR_DATA_TABLE_ENTRY_S {
    LIST_ENTRY     InLoadOrderLinks;
    LIST_ENTRY     InMemoryOrderLinks;
    LIST_ENTRY     InInitializationOrderLinks;
    PVOID          DllBase;
    PVOID          EntryPoint;
    ULONG          SizeOfImage;
    UNICODE_STRING FullDllName;
    UNICODE_STRING BaseDllName;
} LDR_DATA_TABLE_ENTRY_S, *PLDR_DATA_TABLE_ENTRY_S;

typedef struct _PEB_S { UCHAR pad[0x18]; PPEB_LDR_DATA_S Ldr; } PEB_S, *PPEB_S;

// ==========================================================================
//                            Primitive Ops (1:1 original)
// ==========================================================================

// ReadVirtualMemory — cross-process copy via MmCopyVirtualMemory.
// Original does NOT attach; MmCopyVirtualMemory handles that internally.
static NTSTATUS ReadVirtualMemory(
    _In_ PEPROCESS target, _In_ PVOID srcVA, _Out_ PVOID dst, _In_ SIZE_T size)
{
    if (!target || !srcVA || !dst || !size) return STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER;
    SIZE_T copied = 0;
    NTSTATUS s = STATUS_UNSUCCESSFUL;
    __try {
        s = MmCopyVirtualMemory(target, srcVA, PsGetCurrentProcess(), dst, size, KernelMode, &copied);
    } __except (EXCEPTION_EXECUTE_HANDLER) { s = GetExceptionCode(); }
    return s;
}

static NTSTATUS WriteVirtualMemory(
    _In_ PEPROCESS target, _In_ PVOID dstVA, _In_ PVOID src, _In_ SIZE_T size)
{
    if (!target || !dstVA || !src) return STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER;
    SIZE_T copied = 0;
    NTSTATUS s = STATUS_UNSUCCESSFUL;
    __try {
        s = MmCopyVirtualMemory(PsGetCurrentProcess(), src, target, dstVA, size, KernelMode, &copied);
    } __except (EXCEPTION_EXECUTE_HANDLER) { s = GetExceptionCode(); }
    return s;
}

// AllocateVirtualMemory — mirrors original sub_140001AB0.
// KeStackAttachProcess then ZwAllocateVirtualMemory with pseudo-handle -1
// (NtCurrentProcess()) so the allocation lands in the *attached* process.
static NTSTATUS AllocateVirtualMemory(
    _In_ PEPROCESS target, _Inout_ PVOID* pBase, _In_ SIZE_T size,
    _In_ ULONG allocType, _In_ ULONG protect)
{
    if (!target || !pBase) return STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER;

    KAPC_STATE apc;
    KeStackAttachProcess(target, &apc);

    PVOID  base  = *pBase;
    SIZE_T rsize = size;
    NTSTATUS s = STATUS_UNSUCCESSFUL;
    __try {
        s = ZwAllocateVirtualMemory(
            (HANDLE)(LONG_PTR)-1, &base, 0, &rsize, allocType, protect);
    } __except (EXCEPTION_EXECUTE_HANDLER) { s = GetExceptionCode(); }

    KeUnstackDetachProcess(&apc);
    if (NT_SUCCESS(s)) *pBase = base;
    return s;
}

// ProtectVirtualMemory — mirrors original sub_140001F30.
static NTSTATUS ProtectVirtualMemory(
    _In_ PEPROCESS target, _Inout_ PVOID* pBase, _Inout_ PSIZE_T pSize,
    _In_ ULONG newProtect, _Out_ PULONG oldProtect)
{
    if (!target || !pBase || !pSize || !oldProtect) return STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER;

    KAPC_STATE apc;
    KeStackAttachProcess(target, &apc);

    NTSTATUS s = STATUS_UNSUCCESSFUL;
    __try {
        s = ZwProtectVirtualMemory(
            (HANDLE)(LONG_PTR)-1, pBase, pSize, newProtect, oldProtect);
    } __except (EXCEPTION_EXECUTE_HANDLER) { s = GetExceptionCode(); }

    KeUnstackDetachProcess(&apc);
    return s;
}

// GetModuleBase — walks the target's PEB while attached.
// Returns DllBase of BaseDllName == ModuleName (case-insensitive).
static PVOID GetModuleBase(_In_ PEPROCESS target, _In_ PCWSTR moduleName)
{
    if (!target || !moduleName) return nullptr;

    KAPC_STATE apc;
    KeStackAttachProcess(target, &apc);

    PVOID base = nullptr;
    __try {
        auto peb = reinterpret_cast<PPEB_S>(PsGetProcessPeb(target));
        if (peb && peb->Ldr) {
            UNICODE_STRING want;
            RtlInitUnicodeString(&want, moduleName);
            auto* head = &peb->Ldr->InLoadOrderModuleList;
            LIST_ENTRY* e = head->Flink;
            // sanity: head->Flink must be non-null; loop bounded to avoid
            // infinite iteration on corrupted list.
            for (ULONG i = 0; e && e != head && i < 4096; ++i, e = e->Flink) {
                auto entry = CONTAINING_RECORD(e, LDR_DATA_TABLE_ENTRY_S, InLoadOrderLinks);
                if (entry->BaseDllName.Buffer && entry->BaseDllName.Length &&
                    RtlEqualUnicodeString(&entry->BaseDllName, &want, TRUE)) {
                    base = entry->DllBase;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    } __except (EXCEPTION_EXECUTE_HANDLER) { base = nullptr; }

    KeUnstackDetachProcess(&apc);
    return base;
}

// CreateUserThread — mirrors ORIGINAL sub_140001BE8 exactly.
// KeStackAttachProcess + ZwCreateThreadEx(pseudo-handle -1) OR
// RtlCreateUserThread fallback if ZwCreateThreadEx unavailable.
static NTSTATUS CreateUserThread(
    _In_ PEPROCESS target, _In_ PVOID startRoutine, _In_ PVOID parameter)
{
    if (!target || !startRoutine) return STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER;

    KAPC_STATE apc;
    HANDLE hThread = nullptr;
    NTSTATUS s = STATUS_UNSUCCESSFUL;

    KeStackAttachProcess(target, &apc);

    __try {
        if (g_ZwCreateThreadEx) {
            s = g_ZwCreateThreadEx(
                &hThread,
                0x1FFFFF,               // THREAD_ALL_ACCESS
                nullptr,
                (HANDLE)(LONG_PTR)-1,   // pseudo-handle == attached process
                startRoutine,
                parameter,
                0, 0, 0, 0, nullptr);
        } else if (g_RtlCreateUserThread) {
            UCHAR clientId[16] = {0};
            s = g_RtlCreateUserThread(
                (HANDLE)(LONG_PTR)-1, nullptr, FALSE, 0, nullptr, nullptr,
                startRoutine, parameter, &hThread, clientId);
        } else {
            s = STATUS_NOT_FOUND;
        }
    } __except (EXCEPTION_EXECUTE_HANDLER) { s = GetExceptionCode(); }

    KeUnstackDetachProcess(&apc);

    if (NT_SUCCESS(s) && hThread) {
        ZwClose(hThread);
    }
    return s;
}

// ==========================================================================
//                              IOCTL Dispatch
// ==========================================================================

NTSTATUS Dispatch(PDEVICE_OBJECT dev, PIRP irp)
{
    UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(dev);
    auto* sp = IoGetCurrentIrpStackLocation(irp);
    NTSTATUS s = STATUS_SUCCESS;
    ULONG_PTR info = 0;

    if (sp->MajorFunction == IRP_MJ_CREATE || sp->MajorFunction == IRP_MJ_CLOSE)
        goto done;

    if (sp->MajorFunction != IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL) {
        s = STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST;
        goto done;
    }

    {
        const ULONG code   = sp->Parameters.DeviceIoControl.IoControlCode;
        const ULONG inLen  = sp->Parameters.DeviceIoControl.InputBufferLength;
        const ULONG outLen = sp->Parameters.DeviceIoControl.OutputBufferLength;
        PVOID buf = irp->AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer;

        // All IOCTLs require a non-null buffer.
        if (!buf) { s = STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER; goto done; }

        switch (code) {

        // ---------- SELF_TEST -----------------------------------------------
        case IOCTL_HEXSYNC_SELF_TEST: {
            if (outLen < sizeof(HEXSYNC_SELFTEST)) { s = STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL; break; }
            auto* out = static_cast<PHEXSYNC_SELFTEST>(buf);
            out->MagicLow  = HEXSYNC_SELF_TEST_MAGIC_LOW;
            out->MagicHigh = HEXSYNC_SELF_TEST_MAGIC_HIGH;
            info = sizeof(HEXSYNC_SELFTEST);
            break;
        }

        // ---------- READ_MEMORY ---------------------------------------------
        case IOCTL_HEXSYNC_READ_MEMORY: {
            if (inLen < sizeof(HEXSYNC_READ_REQ)) { s = STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL; break; }
            auto* req = static_cast<PHEXSYNC_READ_REQ>(buf);
            if (outLen < req->Size) { s = STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL; break; }

            PEPROCESS target = nullptr;
            s = PsLookupProcessByProcessId((HANDLE)(ULONG_PTR)req->Pid, &target);
            if (!NT_SUCCESS(s)) break;

            // Save Size before overwriting buffer via ReadVirtualMemory (in/out share buffer).
            SIZE_T copySize = (SIZE_T)req->Size;
            PVOID  srcVA    = req->RemoteAddress;
            s = ReadVirtualMemory(target, srcVA, buf, copySize);
            ObDereferenceObject(target);
            if (NT_SUCCESS(s)) info = copySize;
            break;
        }

        // ---------- WRITE_MEMORY --------------------------------------------
        case IOCTL_HEXSYNC_WRITE_MEMORY: {
            if (inLen < sizeof(HEXSYNC_WRITE_REQ)) { s = STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL; break; }
            auto* req = static_cast<PHEXSYNC_WRITE_REQ>(buf);
            if (inLen < sizeof(*req) + req->Size) { s = STATUS_INFO_LENGTH_MISMATCH; break; }
            // Guard against integer overflow on req->Size + header.
            if (req->Size > 0xFFFFFFFFULL) { s = STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER; break; }

            PEPROCESS target = nullptr;
            s = PsLookupProcessByProcessId((HANDLE)(ULONG_PTR)req->Pid, &target);
            if (!NT_SUCCESS(s)) break;

            PUCHAR payload = reinterpret_cast<PUCHAR>(req) + sizeof(*req);
            s = WriteVirtualMemory(target, req->RemoteAddress, payload, (SIZE_T)req->Size);
            ObDereferenceObject(target);
            info = sizeof(*req);
            break;
        }

        // ---------- GET_MODULE_BASE -----------------------------------------
        case IOCTL_HEXSYNC_GET_MODULE_BASE: {
            if (inLen < sizeof(HEXSYNC_MODULE_REQ) || outLen < sizeof(HEXSYNC_MODULE_REQ)) {
                s = STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL; break;
            }
            auto* req = static_cast<PHEXSYNC_MODULE_REQ>(buf);

            PEPROCESS target = nullptr;
            s = PsLookupProcessByProcessId((HANDLE)(ULONG_PTR)req->Pid, &target);
            if (!NT_SUCCESS(s)) break;

            // Ensure ModuleName is null-terminated within its 260-wchar bound.
            req->ModuleName[259] = 0;
            req->OutBase = GetModuleBase(target, req->ModuleName);
            ObDereferenceObject(target);
            info = sizeof(*req);
            break;
        }

        // ---------- ALLOCATE_MEMORY -----------------------------------------
        case IOCTL_HEXSYNC_ALLOCATE_MEMORY: {
            if (inLen < sizeof(HEXSYNC_ALLOC_REQ) || outLen < sizeof(HEXSYNC_ALLOC_REQ)) {
                s = STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL; break;
            }
            auto* req = static_cast<PHEXSYNC_ALLOC_REQ>(buf);

            PEPROCESS target = nullptr;
            s = PsLookupProcessByProcessId((HANDLE)(ULONG_PTR)req->Pid, &target);
            if (!NT_SUCCESS(s)) break;

            const ULONG allocType = req->AllocType ? req->AllocType : (MEM_COMMIT | MEM_RESERVE);
            const ULONG protect   = req->Protect   ? req->Protect   : PAGE_READWRITE;
            PVOID base = req->Address;
            s = AllocateVirtualMemory(target, &base, (SIZE_T)req->Size, allocType, protect);
            ObDereferenceObject(target);
            if (NT_SUCCESS(s)) {
                req->Address = base;
                info = sizeof(*req);
            }
            break;
        }

        // ---------- PROTECT_MEMORY ------------------------------------------
        case IOCTL_HEXSYNC_PROTECT_MEMORY: {
            if (inLen < sizeof(HEXSYNC_PROTECT_REQ) || outLen < sizeof(HEXSYNC_PROTECT_REQ)) {
                s = STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL; break;
            }
            auto* req = static_cast<PHEXSYNC_PROTECT_REQ>(buf);

            PEPROCESS target = nullptr;
            s = PsLookupProcessByProcessId((HANDLE)(ULONG_PTR)req->Pid, &target);
            if (!NT_SUCCESS(s)) break;

            PVOID  base    = req->RemoteAddress;
            SIZE_T rsize   = (SIZE_T)req->Size;
            ULONG  oldProt = 0;
            s = ProtectVirtualMemory(target, &base, &rsize, req->NewProtect, &oldProt);
            ObDereferenceObject(target);
            if (NT_SUCCESS(s)) {
                req->OldProtect = oldProt;
                info = sizeof(*req);
            }
            break;
        }

        // ---------- CREATE_THREAD -------------------------------------------
        case IOCTL_HEXSYNC_CREATE_THREAD: {
            if (inLen < sizeof(HEXSYNC_THREAD_REQ)) { s = STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL; break; }
            auto* req = static_cast<PHEXSYNC_THREAD_REQ>(buf);

            PEPROCESS target = nullptr;
            s = PsLookupProcessByProcessId((HANDLE)(ULONG_PTR)req->Pid, &target);
            if (!NT_SUCCESS(s)) break;

            s = CreateUserThread(target, req->StartRoutine, req->Parameter);
            ObDereferenceObject(target);
            info = sizeof(*req);
            break;
        }

        // ---------- SECRET (stubbed — original XOR channel, unused) ---------
        case IOCTL_HEXSYNC_GET_SECRET:
        case IOCTL_HEXSYNC_SET_SECRET:
            s = STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED;
            break;

        default:
            s = STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST;
            break;
        }
    }

done:
    irp->IoStatus.Status = s;
    irp->IoStatus.Information = info;
    IoCompleteRequest(irp, IO_NO_INCREMENT);
    return s;
}

VOID DriverUnload(PDRIVER_OBJECT drv)
{
    UNICODE_STRING sym = RTL_CONSTANT_STRING(HEXSYNC_DEVICE_NAME_USER);
    IoDeleteSymbolicLink(&sym);
    if (drv->DeviceObject) IoDeleteDevice(drv->DeviceObject);
}

static void ResolveDynamic()
{
    UNICODE_STRING n1 = RTL_CONSTANT_STRING(L"ZwCreateThreadEx");
    UNICODE_STRING n2 = RTL_CONSTANT_STRING(L"RtlCreateUserThread");
    g_ZwCreateThreadEx    = reinterpret_cast<pfnZwCreateThreadEx>(MmGetSystemRoutineAddress(&n1));
    g_RtlCreateUserThread = reinterpret_cast<pfnRtlCreateUserThread>(MmGetSystemRoutineAddress(&n2));
}

extern "C" NTSTATUS IoCreateDriver(
    _In_opt_ PUNICODE_STRING DriverName,
    _In_     PDRIVER_INITIALIZE InitializationFunction);

extern "C"
NTSTATUS DriverEntry(PDRIVER_OBJECT drv, PUNICODE_STRING reg)
{
    UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(reg);

    // kdmapper-loaded drivers get DriverObject = NULL. The public HexSyncService
    // is a WDF driver loaded as a signed service — its DriverObject is always
    // valid. When we reimplement the driver and load via kdmap, we must ask
    // the kernel to create a real DRIVER_OBJECT for us.
    //
    // IoCreateDriver() allocates a DRIVER_OBJECT, calls our InitializationFunction
    // with a valid pointer, and takes care of unload/reference housekeeping.
    if (!drv) {
        UNICODE_STRING driverName = RTL_CONSTANT_STRING(L"\\Driver\\CS2HexSyncCompat");
        return IoCreateDriver(&driverName, (PDRIVER_INITIALIZE)DriverEntry);
    }

    UNICODE_STRING devName = RTL_CONSTANT_STRING(HEXSYNC_DEVICE_NAME_KERNEL);
    UNICODE_STRING symName = RTL_CONSTANT_STRING(HEXSYNC_DEVICE_NAME_USER);
    PDEVICE_OBJECT devObj = nullptr;

    NTSTATUS s = IoCreateDevice(drv, 0, &devName, FILE_DEVICE_UNKNOWN,
        FILE_DEVICE_SECURE_OPEN, FALSE, &devObj);
    if (!NT_SUCCESS(s)) return s;

    s = IoCreateSymbolicLink(&symName, &devName);
    if (!NT_SUCCESS(s)) { IoDeleteDevice(devObj); return s; }

    devObj->Flags |= DO_BUFFERED_IO;
    devObj->Flags &= ~DO_DEVICE_INITIALIZING;

    drv->MajorFunction[IRP_MJ_CREATE]         = Dispatch;
    drv->MajorFunction[IRP_MJ_CLOSE]          = Dispatch;
    drv->MajorFunction[IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL] = Dispatch;
    drv->DriverUnload = DriverUnload;

    ResolveDynamic();
    return STATUS_SUCCESS;
}

```

`source/drivers/CS2HexSyncCompatDriver/shared.h`:

```h
#pragma once
//
// HexSync-compatible IOCTL contract — byte-for-byte matched to the original
// NLinjector HexSyncService.sys layout (reverse-engineered via IDA MCP).
//
// Layouts verified 2026-07-11 against extracted HexSyncService.sys embedded
// in NLinjector.exe resources. Field offsets (esp. ALLOCATE + MODULE)
// intentionally match the original — DO NOT reorder.
//

// Device name: unique to avoid clashing with the original NLinjector-installed
// HexSyncService (which is a signed WDF service). Users of NLinjector may have
// that service registered already; overlapping names would break both.
#define HEXSYNC_DEVICE_NAME_KERNEL   L"\\Device\\CS2HexSyncCompat"
#define HEXSYNC_DEVICE_NAME_USER     L"\\??\\CS2HexSyncCompat"
#define HEXSYNC_DEVICE_NAME_WIN32    L"\\\\.\\CS2HexSyncCompat"

//
// 9 IOCTL codes (all FILE_DEVICE_UNKNOWN, METHOD_BUFFERED, FILE_ANY_ACCESS)
//
#define IOCTL_HEXSYNC_READ_MEMORY       0x222000
#define IOCTL_HEXSYNC_WRITE_MEMORY      0x222004
#define IOCTL_HEXSYNC_GET_MODULE_BASE   0x222008
#define IOCTL_HEXSYNC_SELF_TEST         0x22200C
#define IOCTL_HEXSYNC_GET_SECRET        0x222010
#define IOCTL_HEXSYNC_SET_SECRET        0x222014
#define IOCTL_HEXSYNC_ALLOCATE_MEMORY   0x222018
#define IOCTL_HEXSYNC_PROTECT_MEMORY    0x22201C
#define IOCTL_HEXSYNC_CREATE_THREAD     0x222020

// SELF_TEST magic: MagicLow (DWORD) at offset 0, MagicHigh (QWORD) at offset 4.
// Reading as ULL from offset 0 → 0x0000000200008421.
#define HEXSYNC_SELF_TEST_MAGIC_LOW    0x8421u
#define HEXSYNC_SELF_TEST_MAGIC_HIGH   0x2u
#define HEXSYNC_SELF_TEST_MAGIC_QWORD  0x200008421ULL

#pragma pack(push, 1)

// READ_MEMORY (0x222000) — 24 B in, Size B out
typedef struct _HEXSYNC_READ_REQ {
    unsigned int         Pid;              // 0
    unsigned int         Padding;          // 4
    void*                RemoteAddress;    // 8
    unsigned long long   Size;             // 16
} HEXSYNC_READ_REQ, *PHEXSYNC_READ_REQ;    // 24 B

// WRITE_MEMORY (0x222004) — 24 + Size B in
typedef struct _HEXSYNC_WRITE_REQ {
    unsigned int         Pid;              // 0
    unsigned int         Padding;          // 4
    void*                RemoteAddress;    // 8
    unsigned long long   Size;             // 16
    // followed by Size bytes of payload (contiguous)
} HEXSYNC_WRITE_REQ, *PHEXSYNC_WRITE_REQ;   // 24 + Size

// GET_MODULE_BASE (0x222008) — 536 B in/out
// Original HexSyncService layout: ModuleName starts at offset 4 (no pad after Pid).
// OutBase written to offset 528 (= 8 * 66).
typedef struct _HEXSYNC_MODULE_REQ {
    unsigned int         Pid;              // 0
    wchar_t              ModuleName[260];  // 4 .. 523 (520 B)
    unsigned int         Reserved;         // 524 (padding to align OutBase)
    void*                OutBase;          // 528 (out)
} HEXSYNC_MODULE_REQ, *PHEXSYNC_MODULE_REQ; // 536 B

// SELF_TEST (0x22200C) — 12 B out
typedef struct _HEXSYNC_SELFTEST {
    unsigned int         MagicLow;         // 0
    unsigned long long   MagicHigh;        // 4 (packed, unaligned)
} HEXSYNC_SELFTEST, *PHEXSYNC_SELFTEST;    // 12 B

// GET_SECRET (0x222010) / SET_SECRET (0x222014) — 260 B / 264 B
typedef struct _HEXSYNC_SECRET {
    unsigned int         Size;             // 0
    unsigned char        Payload[256];     // 4 .. 259
} HEXSYNC_SECRET, *PHEXSYNC_SECRET;

// ALLOCATE_MEMORY (0x222018) — 32 B in/out
// CRITICAL: field order matches ORIGINAL HexSyncService layout:
//   Size at offset 8, AllocType at offset 16, Protect at offset 20, Address at offset 24.
typedef struct _HEXSYNC_ALLOC_REQ {
    unsigned int         Pid;              // 0
    unsigned int         Padding;          // 4
    unsigned long long   Size;             // 8
    unsigned int         AllocType;        // 16
    unsigned int         Protect;          // 20
    void*                Address;          // 24 (in: preferred / out: actual)
} HEXSYNC_ALLOC_REQ, *PHEXSYNC_ALLOC_REQ;   // 32 B

// PROTECT_MEMORY (0x22201C) — 32 B in/out
typedef struct _HEXSYNC_PROTECT_REQ {
    unsigned int         Pid;              // 0
    unsigned int         Padding;          // 4
    void*                RemoteAddress;    // 8
    unsigned long long   Size;             // 16
    unsigned int         NewProtect;       // 24
    unsigned int         OldProtect;       // 28 (out)
} HEXSYNC_PROTECT_REQ, *PHEXSYNC_PROTECT_REQ; // 32 B

// CREATE_THREAD (0x222020) — 24 B in
typedef struct _HEXSYNC_THREAD_REQ {
    unsigned int         Pid;              // 0
    unsigned int         Padding;          // 4
    void*                StartRoutine;     // 8
    void*                Parameter;        // 16
} HEXSYNC_THREAD_REQ, *PHEXSYNC_THREAD_REQ; // 24 B

#pragma pack(pop)

```
